verses.original:
आगें चले बहुरि रघुराया। रिष्यमूक परवत निअराया।।
तहँ रह सचिव सहित सुग्रीवा। आवत देखि अतुल बल सींवा।।
verses.transliteration:
āgeṃ cale bahuri raghurāyā. riṣyamūka parvata niʼarāyā.
tahaṃ raha saciva sahita sugrīvā. āvata dekhi atula bala sīṃvā.
verses.translation: Lord Rama proceeded further and drew near to Mount Rishyamook. There dwelt Sugriva with his ministers. Seeing two beings of matchless strength approaching, he grew alarmed.

verses.original:
अति सभीत कह सुनु हनुमाना। पुरुष जुगल बल रूप निधाना।।
धरि बटु रूप देखु तैं जाई। कहेसु जानि जियँ सयन बुझाई।।
verses.transliteration:
ati sabhīta kaha sunu hanumānā. puruṣa jugala bala rūpa nidhānā.
dhari baṭu rūpa dekhu taiṃ jāī. kahesu jāni jiyaṃ sayana bujhāī.
verses.translation: Greatly frightened, he said, 'Listen Hanuman, these two men are repositories of strength and beauty. Assuming the form of a young Brahmin student, go and see them. Ascertain their intentions and report back wisely.'

verses.original:
पठए बालि होहिं मन मैला। भागौं तुरत तजौं यह सैला।।
बिप्र रूप धरि कपि तहँ गयऊ। माथ नाइ पूछत अस भयऊ।।
verses.transliteration:
paṭhae bāli hohiṃ mana mailā. bhāgauṃ turata tajauṃ yaha sailā.
bipra rūpa dhari kapi tahaṃ gayaū. mātha nāi pūchata asa bhayaū.
verses.translation: 'If they have been sent by Vali with evil intent, I shall flee at once and abandon this mountain.' Taking the form of a Brahmin, the monkey went there, bowed his head and asked them thus:

verses.original:
को तुम्ह स्यामल गौर सरीरा। छत्री रूप फिरहु बन बीरा।।
कठिन भूमि कोमल पद गामी। कवन हेतु बिचरहु बन स्वामी।।
verses.transliteration:
ko tumha syāmala gaura sarīrā. chatrī rūpa phirahu bana bīrā.
kaṭhina bhūmi komala pada gāmī. kavana hetu bicarahu bana svāmī.
verses.translation: 'Who are you — one dark and one fair of body? You roam the forest like warriors in royal guise. Your tender feet tread upon the hard ground — for what reason do you wander in the forest, O lords?'

verses.original:
मृदुल मनोहर सुंदर गाता। सहत दुसह बन आतप बाता।।
की तुम्ह तीनि देव महँ कोऊ। नर नारायन की तुम्ह दोऊ।।
verses.transliteration:
mṛdula manohara suṃdara gātā. sahata dusaha bana ātapa bātā.
kī tumha tīni deva mahaṃ koū. nara nārāyaṇa kī tumha doū.
verses.translation: 'Your bodies are soft, charming and beautiful, yet you endure the unbearable heat and wind of the forest. Are you one of the three supreme gods? Or are you both Nara and Narayana?'

verses.original:
जग कारन तारन भव भंजन धरनी भार।
की तुम्ह अकिल भुवन पति लीन्ह मनुज अवतार।।1।।
verses.transliteration:
jaga kārana tārana bhava bhaṃjana dharanī bhāra.
kī tumha akila bhuvana pati līnha manuja avatāra.
verses.translation: Are you the cause of the universe, the deliverer from worldly existence, the destroyer of earth's burden? Or are you the Lord of all worlds who has taken human form?

verses.original:
कोसलेस दसरथ के जाए । हम पितु बचन मानि बन आए।।
नाम राम लछिमन दौउ भाई। संग नारि सुकुमारि सुहाई।।
verses.transliteration:
kosalesa dasaratha ke jāe. hama pitu bacana māni bana āe.
nāma rāma lacchimana dauu bhāī. saṃga nāri sukumāri suhāī.
verses.translation: 'We are sons of King Dasharatha of Kosala. Obeying our father's command, we have come to the forest. Our names are Rama and Lakshmana, the two brothers. With us is our delicate and beautiful wife.'

verses.original:
इहाँ हरि निसिचर बैदेही। बिप्र फिरहिं हम खोजत तेही।।
आपन चरित कहा हम गाई। कहहु बिप्र निज कथा बुझाई।।
verses.transliteration:
ihāṃ hari nisicara baidehī. bipra phirahiṃ hama khojata tehī.
āpana carita kahā hama gāī. kahahu bipra nija kathā bujhāī.
verses.translation: 'A demon has carried off Vaidehi (Sita) from here. O Brahmin, we wander in search of her. We have told you our story. Now please tell us your own story.'

verses.original:
प्रभु पहिचानि परेउ गहि चरना। सो सुख उमा नहिं बरना।।
पुलकित तन मुख आव न बचना। देखत रुचिर बेष कै रचना।।
verses.transliteration:
prabhu pahicāni pareu gahi caranā. so sukha umā nahiṃ baranā.
pulakita tana mukha āva na bacanā. dekhata rucira beṣa kai racanā.
verses.translation: Recognizing the Lord, Hanuman fell and clasped His feet. That joy, O Uma, cannot be described. His body was thrilled, no words came from his mouth, as he beheld the Lord's beautiful form and attire.

verses.original:
पुनि धीरजु धरि अस्तुति कीन्ही। हरष हृदयँ निज नाथहि चीन्ही।।
मोर न्याउ मैं पूछा साईं। तुम्ह पूछहु कस नर की नाईं।।
verses.transliteration:
puni dhīraju dhari astuti kīnhī. haraṣa hṛdayaṃ nija nāthahi cīnhī.
mora nyāu maiṃ pūchā sāīṃ. tumha pūchahu kasa nara kī nāīṃ.
verses.translation: Then gathering courage, he offered praise. His heart was overjoyed having recognized his Lord. 'It was proper for me to ask, O Master. But why do You ask like an ordinary mortal?'

verses.original: तव माया बस फिरउँ भुलाना। ता ते मैं नहिं प्रभु पहिचाना।।
verses.transliteration: tava māyā basa phiraūṃ bhulānā. tā te maiṃ nahiṃ prabhu pahicānā.
verses.translation: 'Under the spell of Your maya, I wandered deluded. That is why I could not recognize You, O Lord.'

verses.original:
एकु मैं मंद मोहबस कुटिल हृदय अग्यान।
पुनि प्रभु मोहि बिसारेउ दीनबंधु भगवान।।2।।
verses.transliteration:
eku maiṃ maṃda mohabasa kuṭila hṛdaya agyāna.
puni prabhu mohi bisārau dīnabaṃdhu bhagavāna.
verses.translation: I am foolish, deluded, crooked of heart and ignorant. Moreover, the Lord had forgotten me — the friend of the lowly, the Almighty.

verses.original:
जदपि नाथ बहु अवगुन मोरें। सेवक प्रभुहि परै जनि भोरें।।
नाथ जीव तव मायाँ मोहा। सो निस्तरइ तुम्हारेहिं छोहा।।
verses.transliteration:
jadapi nātha bahu avaguna moreṃ. sevaka prabhuhī parai jani bhoreṃ.
nātha jīva tava māyā mohā. so nija dāsa tumhahi mana ṭohā.
verses.translation: 'Even though, O Lord, I have many faults, a servant should never be abandoned by the master. O Lord, knowing me to be Your own, please do not forsake me.'

verses.original:
ता पर मैं रघुबीर दोहाई। जानउँ नहिं कछु भजन उपाई।।
सेवक सुत पति मातु भरोसें। रहइ असोच बनइ प्रभु पोसें।।
verses.transliteration:
tā para maiṃ raghubīra dohāī. jānaūṃ nahiṃ kachu bhajana upāī.
sevaka suta pati patanī jānī. nija aparādha daṃḍa nahiṃ ṭhānī.
verses.translation: 'Moreover, I swear by Lord Rama — I know no method of worship. A servant, a son, and a disciple — these should be treated with compassion even when found at fault.'

verses.original:
अस कहि परेउ चरन अकुलाई। निज तनु प्रगटि प्रीति उर छाई।।
तब रघुपति उठाइ उर लावा। निज लोचन जल सींचि जुड़ावा।।
verses.transliteration:
asa kahi pareu carana akulāī. nija tanu pragaṭi prīti ura chāī.
taba raghupati ura lāyau āī. prabhu nija svarūpa dekhāyau.
verses.translation: Saying this, he fell at Rama's feet in agitation, revealing his true monkey form, his heart overflowing with love. Then the Lord of the Raghus spoke gracious words:

verses.original:
सुनु कपि जियँ मानसि जनि ऊना। तैं मम प्रिय लछिमन ते दूना।।
समदरसी मोहि कह सब कोऊ। सेवक प्रिय अनन्यगति सोऊ।।
verses.transliteration:
sunu kapi jiyaṃ mānasi jani ūnā. taiṃ mama priya lacchimana te dūnā.
samadarsī mohi kahu nahiṃ priyā. saba mama pāda kamala kī riyā.
verses.translation: 'Listen, O monkey, do not feel disheartened. You are twice as dear to Me as Lakshmana. You are equal to Me in all virtues.'

verses.original:
सो अनन्य जाकें असि मति न टरइ हनुमंत।
मैं सेवक सचराचर रूप स्वामि भगवंत।।3।।
verses.transliteration:
so ananya jākeṃ asi mati na ṭarai hanumaṃta.
maiṃ sevaka sacarācara rūpa svāmī bhagavaṃta.
verses.translation: He alone is truly devoted whose mind, O Hanuman, never wavers — 'I am a servant and the Lord pervades all creation in His own form.'

verses.original:
देखि पवन सुत पति अनुकूला। हृदयँ हरष बीती सब सूला।।
नाथ सैल पर कपिपति रहई। सो सुग्रीव दास तव अहई।।
verses.transliteration:
dekhi pavana suta pati anukūlā. hṛdayaṃ haraṣa bītī saba sūlā.
nātha saila para kapipati rahaī. so sugrīva dāsa tava ahaī.
verses.translation: Seeing the son of the wind god favored by the Lord, his heart was filled with joy and all his pain vanished. 'O Lord, on this mountain dwells Sugriva, lord of the monkeys.'

verses.original:
तेहि सन नाथ मयत्री कीजे। दीन जानि तेहि अभय करीजे।।
सो सीता कर खोज कराइहि। जहँ तहँ मरकट कोटि पठाइहि।।
verses.transliteration:
tehi sana nātha mayatrī kīje. dīna jāni tehi abhaya karīje.
so sītā kara khoja karāī. jānehu nātha kahāṃ jahanāhī.
verses.translation: 'Please make friendship with him, O Lord. Knowing him to be helpless, grant him freedom from fear. He will help find Sita's whereabouts and the story of her captivity.'

verses.original:
एहि बिधि सकल कथा समुझाई। लिए दुऔ जन पीठि चढ़ाई।।
जब सुग्रीवँ राम कहुँ देखा। अतिसय जन्म धन्य करि लेखा।।
verses.transliteration:
ehi bidhi sakala kathā samujhāī. lie dauū jana pīṭhi caḍhāī.
jaba sugrīvaṃ rāma kahu dekhā. ura upajā ati haraṣa bisekhā.
verses.translation: In this manner, Hanuman explained everything, then carried the two brothers on his back. When Sugriva saw the two princes approach, he went forward with joy.

verses.original:
सादर मिलेउ नाइ पद माथा। भैंटेउ अनुज सहित रघुनाथा।।
कपि कर मन बिचार एहि रीती। करिहहिं बिधि मो सन ए प्रीती।।
verses.transliteration:
sādara mileu nāi pada māthā. bhaiṃṭeu anuja sahita raghunāthā.
kapi kara mana bichāru asa āvā. rāma kṛpā kara maramu na pāvā.
verses.translation: He met them respectfully, bowing his head at their feet. Lord Rama embraced him along with his younger brother. The monkey's heart was filled with boundless love.

verses.original:
तब हनुमंत उभय दिसि की सब कथा सुनाइ।।
पावक साखी देइ करि जोरी प्रीती दृढ़ाइ।।4।।
verses.transliteration:
taba hanumaṃta ubhaya disi kī saba kathā sunāi.
pāvaka sākhī dei kari jorī prīti dṛḍhāi.
verses.translation: Then Hanuman narrated the full story to both sides, and with fire as witness, a bond of friendship was sealed.

verses.original:
कीन्ही प्रीति कछु बीच न राखा। लछमिन राम चरित सब भाषा।।
कह सुग्रीव नयन भरि बारी। मिलिहि नाथ मिथिलेसकुमारी।।
verses.transliteration:
kīnhī prīti kachu bīca na rākhā. lacchimana rāma carita saba bhāṣā.
kaha sugrīva sunahu raghubīrā. eka bāra ika nāmaka bīrā.
verses.translation: The friendship was made without any reservation. Lakshmana related to Sugriva all of Rama's story. Sugriva said, 'Listen, O merciful Lord, I will tell you my tale.'

verses.original:
मंत्रिन्ह सहित इहाँ एक बारा। बैठ रहेउँ मैं करत बिचारा।।
गगन पंथ देखी मैं जाता। परबस परी बहुत बिलपाता।।
verses.transliteration:
maṃtrinha sahita ihāṃ eka bārā. baiṭha raheuṃ maiṃ karata bicārā.
gagana paṃtha dekha maiṃ jāī. parama bicitra amita chabi chāī.
verses.translation: 'Along with my ministers, I was once sitting here in contemplation. From the sky fell something shining. We saw a great demon carrying a woman through the air.'

verses.original:
राम राम हा राम पुकारी। हमहि देखि दीन्हेउ पट डारी।।
मागा राम तुरत तेहिं दीन्हा। पट उर लाइ सोच अति कीन्हा।।
verses.transliteration:
bahu bicāra kari dīnha dikhāī. āmbuṣa paṭa baṃdhē taba āhī.
nātha eka dina āvā bālī. racchasa eka subhaṭa balavālī.
verses.translation: 'Seeing us, she dropped her ornaments and upper garment wrapped in cloth. These I have kept safe — I will show you, O Lord, and you may identify them.'

verses.original:
कह सुग्रीव सुनहु रघुबीरा। तजहु सोच मन आनहु धीरा।।
सब प्रकार करिहउँ सेवकाई। जेहि बिधि मिलिहि जानकी आई।।
verses.transliteration:
nātha bāli aru maiṃ dvau bhāī. prabhu pratāpa jaga bidita baḍāī.
bālinha mohi mārā ati dīnā. rāja nāri saba sahaja harī līnā.
verses.translation: 'O Lord, my brother Vali seized the kingdom and banished me. He took my wife as well. I live in constant fear on this mountain. He has extraordinary strength.'

verses.original:
सखा बचन सुनि हरषे कृपासिधु बलसींव।
कारन कवन बसहु बन मोहि कहहु सुग्रीव ।।5।।
verses.transliteration:
suni sugrīva bacana raghupati karunā āyasu dīnha.
bāli badhaūṃ tumhahi rāja daūṃ caṃdra prabhu asa kīnha.
verses.translation: Hearing his words, the Lord of the Raghus was moved with compassion and reassured Sugriva, 'I shall slay Vali and restore your kingdom. Have no fear.'

verses.original:
नात बालि अरु मैं द्वौ भाई। प्रीति रही कछु बरनि न जाई।।
मय सुत मायावी तेहि नाऊँ। आवा सो प्रभु हमरें गाऊँ।।
verses.transliteration:
nātha bāli bala atula ati kahi sakauṃ kie pahicāna.
tāsu teja budhi bala pratāpa prabhu jānahu bhagavāna.
verses.translation: 'O Lord, Vali's strength is immense — he has a divine boon. How will You kill one who has such prowess?' Thus spoke Sugriva in doubt.

verses.original:
अर्ध राति पुर द्वार पुकारा। बाली रिपु बल सहै न पारा।।
धावा बालि देखि सो भागा। मैं पुनि गयउँ बंधु सँग लागा।।
verses.transliteration:
eka bāra bāli sana jūjhā. mahiṣāsura duṃdubhī sana būjhā.
bāli bimardeu asa rina dīnhā. tāsu asthinha kara giri eṃka kīnhā.
verses.translation: Once the demon Dundubhi came in the form of a buffalo. A fearsome battle took place between him and Vali. Vali killed him and hurled his carcass a great distance.

verses.original:
गिरिबर गुहाँ पैठ सो जाई। तब बालीं मोहि कहा बुझाई।।
परिखेसु मोहि एक पखवारा। नहिं आवौं तब जानेसु मारा।।
verses.transliteration:
tehi mariṃ muni maṃtaga ke āśrama niarāyau.
muni śrāpa dīnhā bāli kahu yaha bhūmi jauṃ āyau.
verses.translation: The bones of Dundubhi fell near Sage Matanga's hermitage. The sage cursed Vali that he would die if he ever entered this region.

verses.original:
मास दिवस तहँ रहेउँ खरारी। निसरी रुधिर धार तहँ भारी।।
बालि हतेसि मोहि मारिहि आई। सिला देइ तहँ चलेउँ पराई।।
verses.transliteration:
muni ke śrāpa bhaya bāli na āvā. so prabhāva prabhu maiṃ tehi pāvā.
ehi giri basata mohi nahiṃ pīrā. bāli trāsa ati saṃkaṭa bhīrā.
verses.translation: 'Due to the sage's curse, Vali cannot come here. That is why I dwell on this mountain. O Lord, see these trees — Vali could shake them all at once.'

verses.original:
मंत्रिन्ह पुर देखा बिनु साईं। दीन्हेउ मोहि राज बरिआई।।
बालि ताहि मारि गृह आवा। देखि मोहि जियँ भेद बढ़ावा।।
verses.transliteration:
sunu prabhu bāli pratāpa apārā. sāla sāta tehi die ukhārā.
dekhahu nātha dāli taru sākhā. biṭapa bēdhi prabhu prabala na rākhā.
verses.translation: 'O Lord, see these seven Tala trees. Vali would shake them all together with one hand. His power is that immense.'

verses.original:
रिपु सम मोहि मारेसि अति भारी। हरि लीन्हेसि सर्बसु अरु नारी।।
ताकें भय रघुबीर कृपाला। सकल भुवन मैं फिरेउँ बिहाला।।
verses.transliteration:
prabhu eka bāna sāta tāla ūhē. bisika sakala dharani mahaṃ gūhē.
dekhī rāma atulita bala thāhā. sugrīva hṛdaya bharosa upāhā.
verses.translation: Hearing this, the Lord smiled and struck the trees with one arrow. The arrow pierced all seven trees and went through the earth, then returned to His quiver.

verses.original:
इहाँ साप बस आवत नाहीं। तदपि सभीत रहउँ मन माहीं।।
सुनि सेवक दुख दीनदयाला। फरकि उठीं द्वै भुजा बिसाला।।
verses.transliteration:
taba sugrīvaṃ prabhu pada panahī. sādara sapatha kīnha taba tanahī.
nātha bāli kahu māreu morā. tehi biloki maiṃ hona kaṭhorā.
verses.translation: Sugriva was now convinced of Rama's matchless strength. His heart was filled with confidence. He said, 'Now I am certain, O Lord — You alone can slay Vali.'

verses.original:
सुनु सुग्रीव मारिहउँ बालिहि एकहिं बान।
ब्रम्ह रुद्र सरनागत गएँ न उबरिहिं प्रान।।6।।
verses.transliteration:
taba sugrīva mahā dhuni kīnhī. suni bāli krodhavaśa taba līnhī.
āyau bāli krodhātura dhāī. bhireu sugrīva pratāpa baḍāī.
verses.translation: Then Sugriva challenged Vali by roaring loudly. Hearing the challenge, Vali rushed out in great fury to fight.

verses.original:
जे न मित्र दुख होहिं दुखारी। तिन्हहि बिलोकत पातक भारी।।
निज दुख गिरि सम रज करि जाना। मित्रक दुख रज मेरु समाना।।
verses.transliteration:
bāli sugrīva mahā rana hoī. ekahin sana eka mila gae doī.
bhireu dūnahūṃ bala ati bhārī. sira mūṃde rāma nayana nihārī.
verses.translation: Sugriva and Vali fought fiercely. Both looked identical. Rama could not distinguish between them and did not shoot His arrow.

verses.original:
जिन्ह कें असि मति सहज न आई। ते सठ कत हठि करत मिताई।।
कुपथ निवारि सुपंथ चलावा। गुन प्रगटे अवगुनन्हि दुरावा।।
verses.transliteration:
sugrīva bhāgi āyau prabhu pāhīṃ. bāli bale biloki ripu nāhīṃ.
kaha sugrīva sunahu raghubirā. bāli badhe aba cāhia dhīrā.
verses.translation: Defeated, Sugriva fled back to Rama. He complained, 'O Lord, why did You not slay him? I barely escaped with my life.'

verses.original:
देत लेत मन संक न धरई। बल अनुमान सदा हित करई।।
बिपति काल कर सतगुन नेहा। श्रुति कह संत मित्र गुन एहा।।
verses.transliteration:
prabhu kahaṃ sunu bhāī dvau eka samānā. maiṃ pahicāni na sakeu pacānā.
taba prabhu dīnhi sumana kī mālā. pahiri sugrīva caleu tatkālā.
verses.translation: The Lord said, 'Listen brother, you both look exactly alike. I could not tell you apart, that is why I did not shoot. Now take this flower garland — wear it and go fight again.'

verses.original:
आगें कह मृदु बचन बनाई। पाछें अनहित मन कुटिलाई।।
जा कर चित अहि गति सम भाई। अस कुमित्र परिहरेहि भलाई।।
verses.transliteration:
puni dūnahūṃ bhairu bhayāvana.
bāli sugrīva calāvahiṃ pāvana.
verses.translation: Wearing the garland, Sugriva went again and challenged Vali. An intense battle ensued between the brothers.

verses.original:
सेवक सठ नृप कृपन कुनारी। कपटी मित्र सूल सम चारी।।
सखा सोच त्यागहु बल मोरें। सब बिधि घटब काज मैं तोरें।।
verses.transliteration:
tārā kaha sunu pati bṛṣabhketū. bhairu aba saṃga āyau sugrīva.
rāma lacchimana saṃga tehi jānā. nṛpa dasaratha ke suta bhagavānā.
verses.translation: Vali's wife Tara warned him, 'The one with whom Rama has made alliance — do not fight him. Those two are Rama and Lakshmana, sons of Dasharatha.'

verses.original:
कह सुग्रीव सुनहु रघुबीरा। बालि महाबल अति रनधीरा।।
दुंदुभी अस्थि ताल देखराए। बिनु प्रयास रघुनाथ ढहाए।।
verses.transliteration:
bāli kaha suni bhīru priya jāhī. rāma manuṣa kasa marihahiṃ mōhī.
asi pratīti ura dharī prabhāvā. bāli calā mada baḍa baḍabhāvā.
verses.translation: But Vali did not heed her words. Blinded by power and pride, he went to fight. He pounded Sugriva with great blows.

verses.original:
देखि अमित बल बाढ़ी प्रीती। बालि बधब इन्ह भइ परतीती।।
बार बार नावइ पद सीसा। प्रभुहि जानि मन हरष कपीसा।।
verses.transliteration:
taba raghupati cāpa caḍhāī. chāḍā bāna bāli ura lāī.
bāna lāga bāli taba dhāī. parā bhūmi nahiṃ tanu samhārā.
verses.translation: Then Lord Rama drew His bow and released an arrow. It struck Vali in the chest. Vali fell to the ground.

verses.original:
उपजा ग्यान बचन तब बोला। नाथ कृपाँ मन भयउ अलोला।।
सुख संपति परिवार बड़ाई। सब परिहरि करिहउँ सेवकाई।।
verses.transliteration:
dekheṣi rāma taba bāli nihārī. dhanuṣa bāna kara rūpa udārī.
mana bhā prasanna bāli kara āhī. kaha bāli raghupati sana māhī.
verses.translation: Vali saw the Lord standing before him, bow in hand. Though wounded, his mind turned to devotion. He spoke to Rama with folded hands.

verses.original:
ए सब रामभगति के बाधक। कहहिं संत तब पद अवराधक।।
सत्रु मित्र सुख दुख जग माहीं। माया कृत परमारथ नाहीं।।
verses.transliteration:
nātha mora kachu nahiṃ aparādhā. baiṃra nahiṃ kari kīnha agādhā.
byādha iva māryauṃ mohi āī. nātha aba kahahuṃ mohi samujhāī.
verses.translation: 'O Lord, You have shot me like a hunter from hiding. What dharma is this? I was fighting my brother — what offense did I commit against You?'

verses.original:
बालि परम हित जासु प्रसादा। मिलेहु राम तुम्ह समन बिषादा।।
सपनें जेहि सन होइ लराई। जागें समुझत मन सकुचाई।।
verses.transliteration:
sunu bāli eka dharma nṛpālā. kāhu ke nāri sahaja sab bālā.
brāhmana dhenu devasura nāgā. ye saba dadhi jaṃtu yā agha lāgā.
verses.translation: The Lord explained, 'A younger brother's wife is like a daughter. You took your brother's wife — that is a grave sin. I punish those who commit such adharma.'

verses.original:
अब प्रभु कृपा करहु एहि भाँती। सब तजि भजनु करौं दिन राती।।
सुनि बिराग संजुत कपि बानी। बोले बिहँसि रामु धनुपानी।।
verses.transliteration:
suni prabhu bacana bāli mana māhīṃ. bhā prasanna ura aṃtara nāhīṃ.
nātha mohi aba yaha bara dījē. jāte janam janam prabhu pada sījē.
verses.translation: Hearing the Lord's righteous words, Vali's doubt vanished. His heart was filled with devotion. He said, 'O Lord, You are right. I was blinded by my own power.'

verses.original:
जो कछु कहेहु सत्य सब सोई। सखा बचन मम मृषा न होई।।
नट मरकट इव सबहि नचावत। रामु खगेस बेद अस गावत।।
verses.transliteration:
nātha mama yaha maṃgala mūlā. maiṃ raghupati pada kamala amūlā.
suta mama aṃgada nāma subhāū. tehi rākhahu raghubaṃsa nihāū.
verses.translation: 'O Lord, grant me this boon — may I have devotion to Your feet in every birth. My wife, my son Angad — I entrust them all to You.'

verses.original:
लै सुग्रीव संग रघुनाथा। चले चाप सायक गहि हाथा।।
तब रघुपति सुग्रीव पठावा। गर्जेसि जाइ निकट बल पावा।।
verses.transliteration:
prabhu kaha bāli dharahu aba dhīrā. tumhahi na byāpai mama bāna pīrā.
taba bāli raghupati mukha nihārī. bhā paramapada bhagati su āhī.
verses.translation: The Lord placed His hand on Vali's head and said, 'Abandon all fear.' With his eyes fixed on Rama's face, Vali attained the supreme abode.

verses.original:
सुनत बालि क्रोधातुर धावा। गहि कर चरन नारि समुझावा।।
सुनु पति जिन्हहि मिलेउ सुग्रीवा। ते द्वौ बंधु तेज बल सींवा।।
verses.transliteration:
tārā bikala dekhī raghurāī. dīnhi gyāna hari linhā āī.
taba prabhu sugrīva kahu rājā. dīnha bāli kaha kareu samājā.
verses.translation: Tara came and fell at Rama's feet weeping. The Lord consoled her and instructed Sugriva to perform the last rites.

verses.original: कोसलेस सुत लछिमन रामा। कालहु जीति सकहिं संग्रामा।।
verses.transliteration:
sugrīvahi rāja dīnha taba sādara.
aṃgada kumāra kīnha bānadara.
verses.translation: Sugriva was crowned king of Kishkindha. Angad was made the crown prince. The rainy season was approaching.

verses.original:
कह बालि सुनु भीरु प्रिय समदरसी रघुनाथ।
जौं कदाचि मोहि मारहिं तौ पुनि होउँ सनाथ।।7।।
verses.transliteration:
prabhu kaha sugrīva aba sāvana āyau.
sītā khoja kareu bhari pāvana.
hanumaṃta kahu aṃgūṭhī dīnhā. sugrīvahi rāja abhiṣeka kīnhā.
verses.translation: Thus the Lord installed Sugriva on the throne. Giving his ring to Hanuman, He said, 'In the rainy season, devise a plan to search for Sita.'

verses.original:
अस कहि चला महा अभिमानी। तृन समान सुग्रीवहि जानी।।
भिरे उभौ बाली अति तर्जा । मुठिका मारि महाधुनि गर्जा।।
verses.transliteration:
bariṣā kāla megha nabhacāī. garajata lāge parama suhāī.
lāge baraṣana bāridha kārē. sāvana ghora ghora aṃdhiyārē.
verses.translation: Lord Rama dwelt on Mount Pravarshan with Lakshmana. The rainy season came with dark clouds covering the sky.

verses.original:
तब सुग्रीव बिकल होइ भागा। मुष्टि प्रहार बज्र सम लागा।।
मैं जो कहा रघुबीर कृपाला। बंधु न होइ मोर यह काला।।
verses.transliteration:
damini damakata ghana ghanamālā. garjata manahu bājanē tālā.
mahā bṛṣṭi calī calī āī. jala thala eka rūpa hari bhāī.
verses.translation: The Lord described to Lakshmana the beauty of the rainy season — the peacocks dancing, the frogs singing, and the rivers flowing full.

verses.original:
एकरूप तुम्ह भ्राता दोऊ। तेहि भ्रम तें नहिं मारेउँ सोऊ।।
कर परसा सुग्रीव सरीरा। तनु भा कुलिस गई सब पीरा।।
verses.transliteration:
bṛṣṭi calata taba nadīṃ sunāhīṃ. jalu thalu eka saba bhari gayau māhīṃ.
suni lakhana prabhu bacana suhāē. sāvana ke saba guna samujhāē.
verses.translation: Streams and rivers swelled with water. The earth was lush with new green growth. Rama said, 'See how nature rejoices — but without Sita, this beauty pains My heart.'

verses.original:
मेली कंठ सुमन कै माला। पठवा पुनि बल देइ बिसाला।।
पुनि नाना बिधि भई लराई। बिटप ओट देखहिं रघुराई।।
verses.transliteration:
cāri māsa bariṣā ritu bītī. nirmalī saba disi bhā mana prītī.
prabhu mana sītā kī sudhi āhī. baiṭhe rahahiṃ birahā mana māhī.
verses.translation: The Lord waited patiently through the four months of rain, constantly remembering Sita and thinking of ways to find her.

verses.original:
बहु छल बल सुग्रीव कर हियँ हारा भय मानि।
मारा बालि राम तब हृदय माझ सर तानि।।8।।
verses.transliteration:
bariṣā gata sarada ṛtu āī. sugandha puṣpa bahe saba bhāī.
sugrīva bhoga rāja mada bhūlā. prabhu kāja nija dharama na mūlā.
verses.translation: The rainy season passed. Autumn came with its clear skies. But Sugriva, lost in pleasures, forgot his promise to Rama.

verses.original:
परा बिकल महि सर के लागें। पुनि उठि बैठ देखि प्रभु आगें।।
स्याम गात सिर जटा बनाएँ। अरुन नयन सर चाप चढ़ाएँ।।
verses.transliteration:
prabhu kaha anuja sunahu taba jāhū. sugrīva kahu milahu samujhāhū.
bariṣā gayī pratigyā bisarī. mohi sītā kī sudhī na karī.
verses.translation: Rama said to Lakshmana, 'The rains are over, but Sugriva has not come. Go to Kishkindha and remind him of his promise.'

verses.original:
पुनि पुनि चितइ चरन चित दीन्हा। सुफल जन्म माना प्रभु चीन्हा।।
हृदयँ प्रीति मुख बचन कठोरा। बोला चितइ राम की ओरा।।
verses.transliteration:
calē lacchimana parama riṣāē. krodha baḍhata kapi nagara nihāē.
cāpa caḍhāi bhujā bahu phērī. dekhata kapinha dahana janu dērī.
verses.translation: Lakshmana went to Kishkindha in great fury. His eyes were red with anger. Seeing his wrath, the monkeys trembled.

verses.original:
धर्म हेतु अवतरेहु गोसाई। मारेहु मोहि ब्याध की नाई।।
मैं बैरी सुग्रीव पिआरा। अवगुन कबन नाथ मोहि मारा।।
verses.transliteration:
sugrīva suni ati bhaya māhīṃ. tārā bolaī sunu nṛpanāhīṃ.
tārā calī turata taba āī. lacchimana carana pareu samujhāī.
verses.translation: Hearing of Lakshmana's approach, Sugriva was terrified. He sent Tara to pacify Lakshmana. Tara went and bowed at his feet.

verses.original:
अनुज बधू भगिनी सुत नारी। सुनु सठ कन्या सम ए चारी।।
इन्हहि कुद्दष्टि बिलोकइ जोई। ताहि बधें कछु पाप न होई।।
verses.transliteration:
kaha tārā sunu rājakumārā. nātha karahu nahiṃ krodhā apārā.
sugrīva bhoga mada bhūlā rāyā. aba prabhu carana karai tanu māyā.
verses.translation: Tara said, 'O prince, my lord was lost in pleasures and forgot. Please forgive him. He will now fulfill his duty. The entire monkey army is ready.'

verses.original:
मुढ़ तोहि अतिसय अभिमाना। नारि सिखावन करसि न काना।।
मम भुज बल आश्रित तेहि जानी। मारा चहसि अधम अभिमानी।।
verses.transliteration:
sugrīva āyau taba dhāī. pareu carana prabhu kṛpā dekhi bhāī.
lacchimana krodha gayau saba bīta. kaha sunu sugrīva bhaya na mana kīta.
verses.translation: Sugriva came trembling and fell at Lakshmana's feet. Lakshmana's anger subsided seeing his humility. He said, 'Rise, do not fear. Now send search parties in all directions.'

verses.original:
सुनहु राम स्वामी सन चल न चातुरी मोरि।
प्रभु अजहूँ मैं पापी अंतकाल गति तोरि।।9।।
verses.transliteration:
sugrīva sakala kapi ṭolī bolāē. āē sakala dhīra balavānā.
nala nīla aṃgada jāmavaṃtā. āe kapi bhaṭa balavāna hanumaṃtā.
verses.translation: Then Sugriva summoned the great monkey army. Bears and monkeys arrived from all directions — Nala, Nila, Angad, Jambavan, and thousands more.

verses.original:
सुनत राम अति कोमल बानी। बालि सीस परसेउ निज पानी।।
अचल करौं तनु राखहु प्राना। बालि कहा सुनु कृपानिधाना।।
verses.transliteration:
cahu disi kapi ṭolī saba dhāvā. eka māsa māhaṃ saba khoja kāvā.
sugrīva saba kahu āyasu dīnhā. sītā khoja calahu jiyaṃ līnhā.
verses.translation: Sugriva organized the monkeys into groups and sent them in all four directions. To each group he gave a month's time to find Sita and return.

verses.original:
जन्म जन्म मुनि जतनु कराहीं। अंत राम कहि आवत नाहीं।।
जासु नाम बल संकर कासी। देत सबहि सम गति अविनासी।।
verses.transliteration:
dakṣina disi bānara balavānā. aṃgada jāmavaṃta hanumanā.
nala nīla bahuta kapi saṃgā. calē sakala bala rūpa abhaṃgā.
verses.translation: The southern party was the strongest, led by Angad, with Jambavan, Nala, Nila, and Hanuman. Rama called Hanuman aside.

verses.original: मम लोचन गोचर सोइ आवा। बहुरि कि प्रभु अस बनिहि बनावा।।
verses.transliteration:
rāma hanumaṃta kahu nikṭa bolāyau.
mudrikā eka dīnhi samujhāyau.
sītā dekhahu jāi samujhāī. dei mudrikā kahu mama sudhi bhāī.
verses.translation: Rama gave his ring to Hanuman and said, 'Take this ring. When you find Sita, give it to her as a token so she may know you come from Me.'

verses.original:
सो नयन गोचर जासु गुन नित नेति कहि श्रुति गावहीं।
जिति पवन मन गो निरस करि मुनि ध्यान कबहुँक पावहीं।।
मोहि जानि अति अभिमान बस प्रभु कहेउ राखु सरीरही।
अस कवन सठ हठि काटि सुरतरु बारि करिहि बबूरही।।1।।
अब नाथ करि करुना बिलोकहु देहु जो बर मागऊँ।
जेहिं जोनि जन्मौं कर्म बस तहँ राम पद अनुरागऊँ।।
यह तनय मम सम बिनय बल कल्यानप्रद प्रभु लीजिऐ।
गहि बाहँ सुर नर नाह आपन दास अंगद कीजिऐ।।2।।
verses.transliteration:
bahuta dūra sāgara lāṃghana prabhu kaha hanumaṃta.
tuma sama anya na kōū kapi bala buddhi guṇavaṃta.
verses.translation: The Lord then blessed Hanuman and said, 'You alone have the strength to cross the ocean. Have faith, O son of the wind — success is certain.'

verses.original:
राम चरन दृढ़ प्रीति करि बालि कीन्ह तनु त्याग।
सुमन माल जिमि कंठ ते गिरत न जानइ नाग।।10।।
verses.transliteration:
cahu disā kapinha kahu bhejē. prabhu rāmahi mana sītā rējē.
baiṭhē prabhu dhīraja mana dharī. prīti pratīti pratāpa na ṭarī.
verses.translation: Thus sending the monkey army in all directions, the Lord seated Himself on the mountain, waiting with faith in His devotees.

verses.original:
राम बालि निज धाम पठावा। नगर लोग सब ब्याकुल धावा।।
नाना बिधि बिलाप कर तारा। छूटे केस न देह सँभारा।।
verses.transliteration:
dakṣina disi gae kapi saṃghā. giri bana nadīṃ bikaṭa bahu raṃgā.
sītā khojata phirata udāsā. mile na kaṃhū tabahiṃ mana trāsā.
verses.translation: The southern search party traveled far and wide through dense forests, over mountains, and across rivers, but could not find any trace of Sita.

verses.original:
तारा बिकल देखि रघुराया । दीन्ह ग्यान हरि लीन्ही माया।।
छिति जल पावक गगन समीरा। पंच रचित अति अधम सरीरा।।
verses.transliteration:
aṃgada kaha sunu kapi samājā. māsa bīta gayau bahuta dina lājā.
binu sītā sudhi jauṃ hama jāhīṃ. sugrīva māri hamahi saba pāhīṃ.
verses.translation: Angad said, 'The month is ending. If we return without news, Sugriva will have us killed. And without Sita's news, Lord Rama will be grieved.'

verses.original:
प्रगट सो तनु तव आगें सोवा। जीव नित्य केहि लगि तुम्ह रोवा।।
उपजा ग्यान चरन तब लागी। लीन्हेसि परम भगति बर मागी।।
verses.transliteration:
bahuta bicāra kari saba baiṭhē. taba eka guphā dekhī taba ṭhāḍhē.
pachī ūḍata bheṃjē paṃkhā. āvahiṃ guphā māṃhu asa raṃkhā.
verses.translation: The monkeys were filled with despair. They sat down, wondering what to do. Then they saw a great cave from which birds emerged, their wings wet.

verses.original:
उमा दारु जोषित की नाई। सबहि नचावत रामु गोसाई।।
तब सुग्रीवहि आयसु दीन्हा। मृतक कर्म बिधिबत सब कीन्हा।।
verses.transliteration:
kapi saba guphā māṃhu taba gae. eka sundara ṭhāma taba pae.
svayaṃprabhā nāma tapasvini soī. rāma bhagati mana krama bacanī hoī.
verses.translation: They entered the cave and found a beautiful garden. There they met Swayamprabha, a divine woman devoted to Rama, who offered them food and rest.

verses.original:
राम कहा अनुजहि समुझाई। राज देहु सुग्रीवहि जाई।।
रघुपति चरन नाइ करि माथा। चले सकल प्रेरित रघुनाथा।।
verses.transliteration:
svayaṃprabhā saba kapi pahiṃcāē. sāgara tīra sakala taba āē.
dekheu sāgara atula apārā. baiṭhe saba kapi bhae dukha bhārā.
verses.translation: After resting, Swayamprabha transported them all magically to the ocean shore. They found themselves on the southern coast, facing the vast sea.

verses.original:
लछिमन तुरत बोलाए पुरजन बिप्र समाज।
राजु दीन्ह सुग्रीव कहँ अंगद कहँ जुबराज।।11।।
verses.transliteration:
avadhi bītī saba bhae nirāsā. sagara dekhī baḍhī mana trāsā.
binu sudhi sītā pāi na jāhīṃ. ubhaya bhāṃti kapi mṛtyu nihāhīṃ.
verses.translation: Seeing the limitless ocean before them, the monkeys sat in despair. The month was over. Going back or forward seemed equally hopeless.

verses.original:
उमा राम सम हित जग माहीं। गुरु पितु मातु बंधु प्रभु नाहीं।।
सुर नर मुनि सब कै यह रीती। स्वारथ लागि करहिं सब प्रीती।।
verses.transliteration:
kapi saba baiṭhe karata bicārā. taba āyau gṛdha eka apārā.
nāma saṃpāti jaṭāyu kā bhāī. tāsu carita suni kapi samujhāī.
verses.translation: As the monkeys sat in despair, discussing whether to fast unto death, a great vulture named Sampati appeared. He was the elder brother of Jatayu.

verses.original:
बालि त्रास ब्याकुल दिन राती। तन बहु ब्रन चिंताँ जर छाती।।
सोइ सुग्रीव कीन्ह कपिराऊ। अति कृपाल रघुबीर सुभाऊ।।
verses.transliteration:
jaṭāyu bandhu suni kapi mana piārā. saṃpāti kaha sunu bānara sārā.
rāvana sītahi laṃkā rākhī. laṃkā sata jojana samudra mākhī.
verses.translation: Hearing about Jatayu's sacrifice for Rama, Sampati was deeply moved. He told the monkeys, 'I can see Lanka across the ocean with my divine sight.'

verses.original:
जानतहुँ अस प्रभु परिहरहीं। काहे न बिपति जाल नर परहीं।।
पुनि सुग्रीवहि लीन्ह बोलाई। बहु प्रकार नृपनीति सिखाई।।
verses.transliteration:
suni saṃpāti bacana saba haraṣē. kapi saba ūṭhe carana taba parasē.
saṃpāti kaha sītā maiṃ dekhī. laṃkā māṃhu asoka bana lekhī.
verses.translation: 'Sita is held captive in the Ashoka grove by Ravana in Lanka, which lies a hundred yojanas across the ocean.' Hearing this, the monkeys were overjoyed.

verses.original:
कह प्रभु सुनु सुग्रीव हरीसा। पुर न जाउँ दस चारि बरीसा।।
गत ग्रीषम बरषा रितु आई। रहिहउँ निकट सैल पर छाई।।
verses.transliteration:
sāgara sata jōjana bistārā. kahu kapi kahai kauṃna utārā.
eka kaha maiṃ dasa jojana jāūṃ. eka kaha bīsa pragaṭi bala dhāūṃ.
verses.translation: But who could leap across the vast ocean? One by one, the monkeys declared their limits. 'I can jump ten yojanas,' said one. 'I can do twenty,' said another.

verses.original:
अंगद सहित करहु तुम्ह राजू। संतत हृदय धरेहु मम काजू।।
जब सुग्रीव भवन फिरि आए। रामु प्रबरषन गिरि पर छाए।।
verses.transliteration:
kōū kaha maiṃ sāṭhi tīsā. kōū āsi kōū pacāsā.
jāmavaṃta taba bōlā dhīrā. sunu hanumaṃta tuma bala bīrā.
verses.translation: None could cover the full distance. Then Jambavan, the wise old bear, turned to Hanuman and said, 'Why do you sit silent, O mighty one?'

verses.original:
प्रथमहिं देवन्ह गिरि गुहा राखेउ रुचिर बनाइ।
राम कृपानिधि कछु दिन बास करहिंगे आइ।।12।।
verses.transliteration:
jāmavaṃta kaha sunu hanumaṃtā. tuma sama kapi nahiṃ tīnahu lokaṃtā.
pavana tanaya bala pavana samānā. tuma sahāya raghupati bhagavānā.
verses.translation: Jambavan reminded Hanuman of his forgotten powers: 'You are the son of the Wind God, mighty as your father. Cross this ocean — only you can do it.'

verses.original:
सुंदर बन कुसुमित अति सोभा। गुंजत मधुप निकर मधु लोभा।।
कंद मूल फल पत्र सुहाए। भए बहुत जब ते प्रभु आए ।।
verses.transliteration:
kahaneu janma karma saba terā. rāma kāja kara sahaja saṃberā.
sunu kapi pavanasuta tuma balavaṃtā. bāla kāla tumha keha na gaṃtā.
verses.translation: Jambavan described Hanuman's birth and powers: 'Son of Anjana and the Wind God, you once tried to swallow the sun as a child, thinking it a fruit.'

verses.original:
देखि मनोहर सैल अनूपा। रहे तहँ अनुज सहित सुरभूपा।।
मधुकर खग मृग तनु धरि देवा। करहिं सिद्ध मुनि प्रभु कै सेवा।।
verses.transliteration:
bāla kāla tuma sūrya niharikā. grasana lāge samajhi paka merikā.
iṃdra bajra mārā mukha jāhī. pareu bhūmi taba pavana risāhī.
verses.translation: 'Indra struck you with his thunderbolt, and you fell. The Wind God was enraged and stopped blowing. All creation trembled. Then Brahma revived you with boons.'

verses.original:
मंगलरुप भयउ बन तब ते । कीन्ह निवास रमापति जब ते।।
फटिक सिला अति सुभ्र सुहाई। सुख आसीन तहाँ द्वौ भाई।।
verses.transliteration:
brahma dīnha bahu bara taba tohī. amaratā bala buddhi guna mohī.
aba sunu kapi raghupati kā kājā. sāgara laṃghi jāhu banirājā.
verses.translation: 'You have the strength to leap across the ocean. Remember your powers, O Hanuman. Rama's work must be done — arise!'

verses.original:
कहत अनुज सन कथा अनेका। भगति बिरति नृपनीति बिबेका।।
बरषा काल मेघ नभ छाए। गरजत लागत परम सुहाए।।
verses.transliteration:
suni sarbagya jāmavaṃta bacanā. hanumaṃta hṛdaya bhā racanā.
tanu biṣāla parama haruṣāhī. mānahu parbata kaṃcana āhī.
verses.translation: Hearing Jambavan's words, Hanuman's body began to grow. He remembered his divine powers. His form expanded like a mountain, blazing with energy.

verses.original:
लछिमन देखु मोर गन नाचत बारिद पैखि।
गृही बिरति रत हरष जस बिष्नु भगत कहुँ देखि।।13।।
verses.transliteration:
siddhi sakala raghupati kṛpā jāni hanumaṃta bajraṃga.
prabhu mudrikā maiṃ pāūṃ sītā kari pragaṭa abhigaṃga.
verses.translation: Hanuman grew mighty as a golden mountain, roared with joy, and said, 'I shall leap across the ocean and find Mother Sita! Victory to Lord Rama!'

verses.original:
घन घमंड नभ गरजत घोरा। प्रिया हीन डरपत मन मोरा।।
दामिनि दमक रह न घन माहीं। खल कै प्रीति जथा थिर नाहीं।।
verses.transliteration:
cāḍheu parvata sṛṃga udārā. bhā tanu parama prabhāva apārā.
kapi bala rūpa bahuta baḍa bhāhī. sāgara pāra calana kapi cāhī.
verses.translation: Hanuman climbed to the top of a mountain. His body blazed with power. He readied himself for the great leap across the hundred-yojana ocean.

verses.original:
बरषहिं जलद भूमि निअराएँ। जथा नवहिं बुध बिद्या पाएँ।।
बूँद अघात सहहिं गिरि कैंसें । खल के बचन संत सह जैसें।।
verses.transliteration:
dekhahiṃ kapi saba ati haruṣāhī. prabhu pratāpa bala dhāma samāhī.
ūṃcā bhayau giri sikara samānā. garjana kīnhi parama balavānā.
verses.translation: The monkeys watched in awe as Hanuman stood tall on the mountaintop. His tail curled upward, his face shone bright. He was ready for the impossible feat.

verses.original:
छुद्र नदीं भरि चलीं तोराई। जस थोरेहुँ धन खल इतराई।।
भूमि परत भा ढाबर पानी। जनु जीवहि माया लपटानी।।
verses.transliteration:
ati gabhīra garjanā kīnhī. dharanī dagamagi giri dala dīnhī.
hanumaṃta prabhu mudrikā linhī. sītā khoja calā taba kīnhī.
verses.translation: With a mighty roar that shook the earth, Hanuman sprang into the air. The mountain sank into the ground from the force of his leap.

verses.original:
समिटि समिटि जल भरहिं तलावा। जिमि सदगुन सज्जन पहिं आवा।।
सरिता जल जलनिधि महुँ जाई। होई अचल जिमि जिव हरि पाई।।
verses.transliteration:
jay jay jay hanumaṃta balavaṃtā. jaya raghubīra kṛpā guṇavaṃtā.
sādhu saba bānara haruṣāhī. calā pavana suta bīra samāhī.
verses.translation: The monkeys cheered and shouted, 'Victory to Hanuman! Victory to Lord Rama!' They watched as the son of the wind vanished into the sky over the ocean.

verses.original:
हरित भूमि तृन संकुल समुझि परहिं नहिं पंथ।
जिमि पाखंड बाद तें गुप्त होहिं सदग्रंथ।।14।।
verses.transliteration:
jaya hanumaṃta gyāna guna sāgara jaya kapīsa.
sundara kāṃḍa kahata aba śrī raghupati yaha dīsa.
verses.translation: Thus Hanuman, the mighty son of the Wind God, leapt across the ocean for the sake of Lord Rama. This marks the beginning of the glorious Sundar Kand.

verses.original:
दादुर धुनि चहु दिसा सुहाई। बेद पढ़हिं जनु बटु समुदाई।।
नव पल्लव भए बिटप अनेका। साधक मन जस मिलें बिबेका।।
verses.transliteration:
āgēṃ kathā sunahu saba sajjana bhāī.
bariṣā bītī sarada ritu āī.
nirmala bayāra calī ati sukhadāī.
saba disi phūlēṃ kamala mana bhāī.
verses.translation: The Lord remained on Mount Pravarshan, and the four months of rain passed. When autumn arrived, the skies cleared and the world became beautiful.

verses.original:
अर्क जबास पात बिनु भयऊ। जस सुराज खल उद्यम गयऊ।।
खोजत कतहुँ मिलइ नहिं धूरी। करइ क्रोध जिमि धरमहि दूरी।।
verses.transliteration:
sarada samaya aṃbara nirmalā. camakahiṃ tārāgaṇa māhīṃ calā.
bidhu udaya bhā ati anurāgā. sītā binu prabhu mana bhā na lāgā.
verses.translation: The rivers became clear, the ponds filled with lotuses. The moon shone bright. The world was at peace, but Rama's heart ached for Sita.

verses.original:
ससि संपन्न सोह महि कैसी। उपकारी कै संपति जैसी।।
निसि तम घन खद्योत बिराजा। जनु दंभिन्ह कर मिला समाजा।।
verses.transliteration:
prabhu sītā mukha sarisa nihārā. kamalanayana aṃsu jala dhārā.
kaha lakhana prabhu man mati rākhahu. dharama dhīra guṇa sāgara bhākhahu.
verses.translation: Looking at the clear sky, Rama thought of Sita's face. Looking at the lotuses, He remembered her eyes. Every beautiful thing reminded Him of her.

verses.original:
महाबृष्टि चलि फूटि किआरीं । जिमि सुतंत्र भएँ बिगरहिं नारीं।।
कृषी निरावहिं चतुर किसाना। जिमि बुध तजहिं मोह मद माना।।
verses.transliteration:
nātha bharōsā dhārahu mānā. kapi daḷa karihahiṃ sītā khānā.
sugrīva prabhu sevaka balavānā. karihahiṃ kāja prabhu dīnā bhānā.
verses.translation: Lakshmana consoled the Lord, 'O Brother, You are the ocean of patience and wisdom. Sita will be found. The monkeys have been sent. Have faith.'

verses.original:
देखिअत चक्रबाक खग नाहीं। कलिहि पाइ जिमि धर्म पराहीं।।
ऊषर बरषइ तृन नहिं जामा। जिमि हरिजन हियँ उपज न कामा।।
verses.transliteration:
prabhu kaha satya lakhana tava bānī. mama mana sadā basahiṃ hanumaṃtā.
so kapi karihaiṃ mama sahāyatā. jānahu lakhana santa guṇavaṃtā.
verses.translation: The Lord smiled and said, 'You speak truly, dear brother. My heart knows that Hanuman will succeed. He has the strength, the devotion, and the wisdom.'

verses.original:
बिबिध जंतु संकुल महि भ्राजा। प्रजा बाढ़ जिमि पाइ सुराजा।।
जहँ तहँ रहे पथिक थकि नाना। जिमि इंद्रिय गन उपजें ग्याना।।
verses.transliteration:
asa kahi prabhu mana dhīraja dhārī. karata bicāra baiṭhē sukha bhārī.
rāma kṛpā hanumaṃta balavaṃtā. karihahiṃ kāja prabhu sevā saṃtā.
verses.translation: Thus the Lord waited with patience and faith, knowing that His devotee Hanuman would accomplish the impossible task.

verses.original:
कबहुँ प्रबल बह मारुत जहँ तहँ मेघ बिलाहिं।
जिमि कपूत के उपजें कुल सद्धर्म नसाहिं।।15(क)।।
कबहुँ दिवस महँ निबिड़ तम कबहुँक प्रगट पतंग।
बिनसइ उपजइ ग्यान जिमि पाइ कुसंग सुसंग।।15(ख)।।
verses.transliteration:
asa bichāri mana dhīra kari prabhu rāma gunagāna.
hanumaṃta bala buddhi nidhi gayau laṃkā hanumaṃta.
verses.translation: Lord Rama waited with unwavering faith in Hanuman. Meanwhile, the mighty son of the Wind was already soaring across the ocean towards Lanka.

verses.original:
बरषा बिगत सरद रितु आई। लछिमन देखहु परम सुहाई।।
फूलें कास सकल महि छाई। जनु बरषाँ कृत प्रगट बुढ़ाई।।
verses.transliteration:
sugrīva rāja mada mātā. bhoga bilāsa basē dina rātā.
bhūlā prabhu kāja aru nītā. caliha na karata bicāra punītā.
verses.translation: Meanwhile Sugriva, intoxicated with royal pleasures, forgot his promise. Wine and women had clouded his mind. The autumn month passed.

verses.original:
उदित अगस्ति पंथ जल सोषा। जिमि लोभहि सोषइ संतोषा।।
सरिता सर निर्मल जल सोहा। संत हृदय जस गत मद मोहा।।
verses.transliteration:
prabhu kaha lakhana sunahu hamarā. sugrīva bhūlā kāja hamārā.
bariṣā bītī māsa bhā āhī. sugrīva sudhi kachu leta na tāhī.
verses.translation: Rama noticed the delay and said to Lakshmana, 'The monkeys were to search and return within a month. Sugriva has forgotten his duty.'

verses.original:
रस रस सूख सरित सर पानी। ममता त्याग करहिं जिमि ग्यानी।।
जानि सरद रितु खंजन आए। पाइ समय जिमि सुकृत सुहाए।।
verses.transliteration:
lacchimana kaha nātha mamai ā. calauṃ kiṣkiṃdhā aba na rahā.
sugrīva yadi nahiṃ āvai sādara. bāli gati hoī sunu raghunadara.
verses.translation: Lakshmana picked up his bow in fury and said, 'I shall go to Kishkindha. If Sugriva does not come to his senses, he shall face the same fate as Vali.'

verses.original:
पंक न रेनु सोह असि धरनी। नीति निपुन नृप कै जसि करनी।।
जल संकोच बिकल भइँ मीना। अबुध कुटुंबी जिमि धनहीना।।
verses.transliteration:
prabhu kaha jāhu bujhāvahu tāhī. dvau pragāra bola kaṭu na kāhī.
lakhana calē kapi nagara nihārī. krodhavaśa mukha bayāna praṭhārī.
verses.translation: Rama said, 'Go, but use gentle words first. He is our ally. Remind him of his promise and his duty.'

verses.original:
बिनु धन निर्मल सोह अकासा। हरिजन इव परिहरि सब आसा।।
कहुँ कहुँ बृष्टि सारदी थोरी। कोउ एक पाव भगति जिमि मोरी।।
verses.transliteration:
calē lakhana ati krodha baḍhāyau. kapi nagara nikaṭa taba āyau.
kapi samadhāna calāē dhāī. sugrīva suni ati bhaya mana āhī.
verses.translation: Lakshmana marched toward Kishkindha. The earth trembled under his angry steps. Seeing him approach, the monkey guards ran to warn Sugriva.

verses.original:
चले हरषि तजि नगर नृप तापस बनिक भिखारि।
जिमि हरिभगत पाइ श्रम तजहि आश्रमी चारि।।16।।
verses.transliteration:
suni lakhana āgavanu sugrīva sahāyau.
ati bhaya mana saba bhoga bisarāyau.
prabhu kāja bisarāyau mada mātā. aba lacchimana āē ripu ghātā.
verses.translation: Hearing of Lakshmana's furious approach, Sugriva was filled with terror. He realized his grave mistake in forgetting Lord Rama's mission.

verses.original:
सुखी मीन जे नीर अगाधा। जिमि हरि सरन न एकउ बाधा।।
फूलें कमल सोह सर कैसा। निर्गुन ब्रम्ह सगुन भएँ जैसा।।
verses.transliteration:
sugrīva tārā taba paṭhāī. parama binaya kari āī.
prabhu pada paduma bāra bāra sīsā. nacāvata tana kaṃpata dīsā.
verses.translation: Sugriva sent Tara and Hanuman's son to pacify Lakshmana. Tara came forward and bowed with humility and grace.

verses.original:
गुंजत मधुकर मुखर अनूपा। सुंदर खग रव नाना रूपा।।
चक्रबाक मन दुख निसि पैखी। जिमि दुर्जन पर संपति देखी।।
verses.transliteration:
tārā kaha sunu rājakumārā. sugrīva devā māna hamārā.
bhoga mada nara sahaja svabhāvā. prabhu aparādha chamahu raghu rāvā.
verses.translation: Tara spoke wisely, 'O prince, forgive the king's lapse. Time lost in pleasure is the nature of mortals. But his devotion to Rama is true.'

verses.original:
चातक रटत तृषा अति ओही। जिमि सुख लहइ न संकरद्रोही।।
सरदातप निसि ससि अपहरई। संत दरस जिमि पातक टरई।।
verses.transliteration:
lakhana krodha taji tārā bacana suni. sugrīva taba āyau dhāī.
carana pareu ati bhaya akūlāhī. chamahu nātha aparādhī nāhī.
verses.translation: Lakshmana's anger softened at Tara's words. Then Sugriva came himself, trembling, and fell at Lakshmana's feet.

verses.original:
देखि इंदु चकोर समुदाई। चितवतहिं जिमि हरिजन हरि पाई।।
मसक दंस बीते हिम त्रासा। जिमि द्विज द्रोह किएँ कुल नासा।।
verses.transliteration:
lacchimana kaha uṭhahu mana dhīrā. āyasu bhayau sunu kapikula bīrā.
calahu turanta karahu aba kājā. bhejahu dūta sakala bana rājā.
verses.translation: Lakshmana said, 'Rise, O king. I was only reminding you of your duty. Now fulfill your promise — summon the army and search for Sita without delay.'

verses.original:
भूमि जीव संकुल रहे गए सरद रितु पाइ।
सदगुर मिले जाहिं जिमि संसय भ्रम समुदाइ।।17।।
verses.transliteration:
sugrīva bolāē kapi koṭinha. āē bānara bhālu akoṭinha.
saba kaha āyasu dīnhā rāyā. calahu turanta karahu raghurāyā.
verses.translation: Sugriva immediately summoned the monkey forces from all corners of the earth. Millions of bears and monkeys assembled, ready for Rama's service.

verses.original:
बरषा गत निर्मल रितु आई। सुधि न तात सीता कै पाई।।
एक बार कैसेहुँ सुधि जानौं। कालहु जीत निमिष महुँ आनौं।।
verses.transliteration:
cahu disi bhejē kapi balavaṃtā. utara dakṣiṇa pūraba hanumantā.
saba kahu āyasu dīnha su āhī. eka māsa mahaṃ āvahu bhāī.
verses.translation: The monkey armies were organized and dispatched in four directions — north, south, east and west — each led by a capable commander.

verses.original:
कतहुँ रहउ जौं जीवति होई। तात जतन करि आनेउँ सोई।।
सुग्रीवहुँ सुधि मोरि बिसारी। पावा राज कोस पुर नारी।।
verses.transliteration:
sugrīva sakala saṃbhāṣana kīnhā. sītā khoja kāja saba līnhā.
bana giri nadīṃ tāla taḍāgā. saba mahaṃ khojahu rāma ke bhāgā.
verses.translation: To each party Sugriva gave detailed instructions: which lands to search, which mountains to cross, and what signs to look for.

verses.original:
जेहिं सायक मारा मैं बाली। तेहिं सर हतौं मूढ़ कहँ काली।।
जासु कृपाँ छूटहीं मद मोहा। ता कहुँ उमा कि सपनेहुँ कोहा।।
verses.transliteration:
dakṣiṇa disi prabala daḷa bhārī. aṃgada nīla nala jāmavaṃta bārī.
hanumaṃta saba mahaṃ balavānā. prabhu kāja kari sakahiṃ sujānā.
verses.translation: The southern party was the strongest and most important. Led by Angad, it included Jambavan, Nala, Nila, and the mighty Hanuman.

verses.original:
जानहिं यह चरित्र मुनि ग्यानी। जिन्ह रघुबीर चरन रति मानी।।
लछिमन क्रोधवंत प्रभु जाना। धनुष चढ़ाइ गहे कर बाना।।
verses.transliteration:
rāma kahā sunu mama mana māhīṃ. tuma sama sevaka mōhi kō nāhīṃ.
hanumaṃta lēhu mudrikā mōrī. sītā dēkhi kahahū sudhi tōrī.
verses.translation: Rama placed special faith in the southern party. He gave his signet ring to Hanuman, saying, 'You will find Sita — I am certain of it.'

verses.original:
तब अनुजहि समुझावा रघुपति करुना सींव।।
भय देखाइ लै आवहु तात सखा सुग्रीव।।18।।
verses.transliteration:
rāma carana mana dhari sabahiṃ pragati calē kapi dhāi.
sītā sudhi lāvahu sabahiṃ prabhu āyasu pāi.
verses.translation: With Rama's ring in his hand and Rama's name in his heart, Hanuman set forth with the southern search party. All monkeys bowed to Rama before departing.

verses.original:
इहाँ पवनसुत हृदयँ बिचारा। राम काजु सुग्रीवँ बिसारा।।
निकट जाइ चरनन्हि सिरु नावा। चारिहु बिधि तेहि कहि समुझावा।।
verses.transliteration:
tīna disi kē kapi phiri āē. sītā khoja karata bilakhāē.
kachu sudhi pāī nahiṃ dēkhā. phirē sabahiṃ mana bhayau bisēkhā.
verses.translation: The search parties scoured every direction. Months passed. The north, east and west parties returned without success, their heads bowed in failure.

verses.original:
सुनि सुग्रीवँ परम भय माना। बिषयँ मोर हरि लीन्हेउ ग्याना।।
अब मारुतसुत दूत समूहा। पठवहु जहँ तहँ बानर जूहा।।
verses.transliteration:
dakṣiṇa disi kapi phirahiṃ udāsā. bana giri khojata mana nahiṃ āsā.
aṃgada hṛdaya dhīra nahiṃ dharī. sītā binu dukha māhīṃ parī.
verses.translation: But the southern party pressed on. They crossed dense forests and treacherous mountains. Angad led them with determination, though despair crept into their hearts.

verses.original:
कहहु पाख महुँ आव न जोई। मोरें कर ता कर बध होई।।
तब हनुमंत बोलाए दूता। सब कर करि सनमान बहूता।।
verses.transliteration:
avadhi nikaṭa kapi bhae nirāsā. baiṭhe sabahiṃ karī mana trāsā.
aṃgada kaha sunu kapi samudāī. prāna tajahu hama nahiṃ ghari jāī.
verses.translation: As the deadline approached with no trace of Sita, the monkeys contemplated giving up their lives. Angad suggested they should fast unto death rather than return empty-handed.

verses.original:
भय अरु प्रीति नीति देखाई। चले सकल चरनन्हि सिर नाई।।
एहि अवसर लछिमन पुर आए। क्रोध देखि जहँ तहँ कपि धाए।।
verses.transliteration:
tabahiṃ saṃpāti gṛdha taba āyau. suni kapi kathā sabahiṃ samujhāyau.
laṃkā sītā rāvana rākhī. sata jojana sāgara bahu sākhī.
verses.translation: It was then that they encountered the great vulture Sampati, brother of Jatayu, who with his divine vision revealed that Sita was held captive in Lanka across the ocean.

verses.original:
धनुष चढ़ाइ कहा तब जारि करउँ पुर छार।
ब्याकुल नगर देखि तब आयउ बालिकुमार।।19।।
verses.transliteration:
suni saṃpāti bacana kapi haraṣē. nahiṃ kachu sudhi pāvata taba paraṣē.
sāgara sata jojana bistārā. koū na tari sakai biṣamata bhārā.
verses.translation: Sampati's revelation filled the monkeys with hope. Now they knew where Sita was — but the vast ocean lay between them and Lanka. Who could cross it?

verses.original:
चरन नाइ सिरु बिनती कीन्ही। लछिमन अभय बाँह तेहि दीन्ही।।
क्रोधवंत लछिमन सुनि काना। कह कपीस अति भयँ अकुलाना।।
verses.transliteration:
baiṭhē kapi sabahiṃ biṣādā. eka eka kaha nija nija pratigyā.
dasa jojana kōū kaha jāūṃ. bīsa tīsa kōū kaha dhāūṃ.
verses.translation: The monkeys sat at the ocean shore, each declaring how far they could leap. 'I can go ten yojanas,' said one. Another said twenty, another thirty.

verses.original:
सुनु हनुमंत संग लै तारा। करि बिनती समुझाउ कुमारा।।
तारा सहित जाइ हनुमाना। चरन बंदि प्रभु सुजस बखाना।।
verses.transliteration:
sata jojana kōū kapi na sakāhī. jāmavaṃta taba cintā māhīṃ.
dekhahiṃ hanumaṃta baiṭhē cūpā. mānahu na jānahiṃ nija anūpā.
verses.translation: But a hundred yojanas was beyond them all. The mighty Jambavan, eldest and wisest, looked at Hanuman who sat quietly in a corner.

verses.original:
करि बिनती मंदिर लै आए। चरन पखारि पलँग बैठाए।।
तब कपीस चरनन्हि सिरु नावा। गहि भुज लछिमन कंठ लगावा।।
verses.transliteration:
jāmavaṃta kaha sunu hanumaṃtā. tuma sama atula na kōū balabaṃtā.
tuma kahuṃ mama katha kahi sunāūṃ. tumharā bala tuma kahuṃ samujhāūṃ.
verses.translation: Jambavan spoke to Hanuman, 'O son of the Wind, why do you sit silent? You do not know your own immeasurable strength. Let me remind you.'

verses.original:
नाथ बिषय सम मद कछु नाहीं। मुनि मन मोह करइ छन माहीं।।
सुनत बिनीत बचन सुख पावा। लछिमन तेहि बहु बिधि समुझावा।।
verses.transliteration:
bāla kāla tumha sūrya nihārā. līlahiṃ calē grasana bistārā.
iṃdra bajra mukha māryau tohī. taba brahma baru dīnha saba mohī.
verses.translation: Jambavan reminded Hanuman of his childhood: 'As a baby, you leapt toward the sun. Indra's thunderbolt could not destroy you. Brahma gave you countless boons.'

verses.original: पवन तनय सब कथा सुनाई। जेहि बिधि गए दूत समुदाई।।
verses.transliteration:
suni saba kathā hanumaṃta nihārā. tanu bisāla bhā parama udārā.
giri samāna bhā ati balavānā. garjā prabhu raghupati guṇa gānā.
verses.translation: Hearing Jambavan's words, Hanuman recalled his powers. His body began to expand. He grew massive as a mountain, blazing with divine energy.

verses.original:
हरषि चले सुग्रीव तब अंगदादि कपि साथ।
रामानुज आगें करि आए जहँ रघुनाथ।।20।।
verses.transliteration:
mai sugrīva sēvaka raghupati kā. sāgara tari jāūṃ ati sīkā.
jaya raghubīra kahi kapi saba haraṣē. hanumaṃta pragaṭa prabhāva na maraṣē.
verses.translation: Hanuman roared, 'I shall leap across this ocean as easily as a lion leaps a stream! Victory to Lord Rama!' The monkeys cheered with boundless joy.

verses.original:
नाइ चरन सिरु कह कर जोरी। नाथ मोहि कछु नाहिन खोरी।।
अतिसय प्रबल देव तब माया। छूटइ राम करहु जौं दाया।।
verses.transliteration:
caḍheu mahēṃdra giri sikharā. tana bala pragaṭa atula ura bharā.
giri ḍōlata kapi bala bisālā. garjā mahā atula pratāpa kālā.
verses.translation: Hanuman climbed to the peak of Mount Mahendra. His golden form blazed like the rising sun. The mountain trembled under his weight.

verses.original:
बिषय बस्य सुर नर मुनि स्वामी। मैं पावँर पसु कपि अति कामी।।
नारि नयन सर जाहि न लागा। घोर क्रोध तम निसि जो जागा।।
verses.transliteration:
mana mahaṃ rāma carana sira nāyau. pavana piṭā kahu sādara dhyāyau.
ūṃcā bhā giri sikara samānā. pragaṭā parama pratāpa hanumaṃnā.
verses.translation: He bowed to Lord Rama in his heart and remembered his father, the Wind God. Then with a mighty leap, he sprang into the sky.

verses.original:
लोभ पाँस जेहिं गर न बँधाया। सो नर तुम्ह समान रघुराया।।
यह गुन साधन तें नहिं होई। तुम्हरी कृपाँ पाव कोइ कोई।।
verses.transliteration:
giri cūra bhā carana prahārā. taru khaga bikala parē bahu dhārā.
laṃghata sāgara ati bala bhārī. calā pavana suta prabhāva apārī.
verses.translation: The force of his leap pushed the mountain deep into the earth. Trees and rocks flew into the air. The ocean waves parted before his mighty form.

verses.original:
तब रघुपति बोले मुसकाई। तुम्ह प्रिय मोहि भरत जिमि भाई।।
अब सोइ जतनु करहु मन लाई। जेहि बिधि सीता कै सुधि पाई।।
verses.transliteration:
surana bikhi nabha puṣpa baraṣāhī. rāma dūta asa kōū na āhī.
pavana tanaya parama balavānā. rāma kāja kari sakahiṃ sujānā.
verses.translation: The gods and sages watched from the sky, showering flowers. 'This is truly the servant of Rama,' they said, 'no ordinary monkey but a divine being.'

verses.original:
एहि बिधि होत बतकही आए बानर जूथ।
नाना बरन सकल दिसि देखिअ कीस बरुथ।।21।।
verses.transliteration: jaya hanumaṃta dēva saba karahiṃ gunagānā. rāma kāja kari sakahiṃ budhi bala hanumaṃtā.
verses.translation: Thus Hanuman, the embodiment of devotion, soared across the sky. The ocean below, the clouds around, and heaven above witnessed his magnificent flight.

verses.original:
बानर कटक उमा में देखा। सो मूरुख जो करन चह लेखा।।
आइ राम पद नावहिं माथा। निरखि बदनु सब होहिं सनाथा।।
verses.transliteration:
jata pavana suta dēvanha dēkhū. laṃghi sāgara atula bala rēkhū.
mainaka sunahi pavana suta āvā. tāsu sēva sagara samujhāvā.
verses.translation: As Hanuman flew over the ocean, Mount Mainaka rose from the water to offer him rest. But Hanuman touched it gently and said, 'I cannot rest until Rama's work is done.'

verses.original:
अस कपि एक न सेना माहीं। राम कुसल जेहि पूछी नाहीं।।
यह कछु नहिं प्रभु कइ अधिकाई। बिस्वरूप ब्यापक रघुराई।।
verses.transliteration:
mainaka kaha baiṭhahu kapi sādara. bisrāma karahu bala buddhi samudara.
hanumaṃta kaha rāma kāja kīnhēṃ. bisrāma karaūṃ yaha praṇa linhēṃ.
verses.translation: Mainaka said, 'The ocean is my father's friend. Please rest a moment.' Hanuman touched the mountain respectfully and said, 'I shall rest after completing Rama's mission.'

verses.original:
ठाढ़े जहँ तहँ आयसु पाई। कह सुग्रीव सबहि समुझाई।।
राम काजु अरु मोर निहोरा। बानर जूथ जाहु चहुँ ओरा।।
verses.transliteration:
surasā āī āsurī māyā. mukha pasārā gagana mahaṃ chāyā.
hanumaṃta budhi bala ati sāgara. laghū rūpa dhari āyau bāhara.
verses.translation: The gods sent Surasa, mother of serpents, to test Hanuman. She opened her mouth wide, saying he must enter it. Hanuman cleverly grew huge, then became tiny and entered and exited her mouth instantly.

verses.original:
जनकसुता कहुँ खोजहु जाई। मास दिवस महँ आएहु भाई।।
अवधि मेटि जो बिनु सुधि पाएँ। आवइ बनिहि सो मोहि मराएँ।।
verses.transliteration:
surasā kaha kapi bala buddhi āhī. rāma kāja tumha sama kōū nāhī.
jāhu siddhi tumhahi saba hoī. rāma kṛpā kachu durlabha na koī.
verses.translation: Surasa blessed him: 'You are truly wise and devoted. You have passed my test. Go forth and succeed in Rama's mission.'

verses.original:
बचन सुनत सब बानर जहँ तहँ चले तुरंत ।
तब सुग्रीवँ बोलाए अंगद नल हनुमंत।।22।।
verses.transliteration:
hanumaṃta calā sāgara pāra. rāma kāja mana dṛḍha bicārā.
laṃkā dīkha ati sundara soī. rāvana gṛha dasa sīsa bisoī.
verses.translation: Having passed Surasa's test, Hanuman flew onward with renewed vigor. The golden shores of Lanka now glimmered on the distant horizon.

verses.original:
सुनहु नील अंगद हनुमाना। जामवंत मतिधीर सुजाना।।
सकल सुभट मिलि दच्छिन जाहू। सीता सुधि पूँछेउ सब काहू।।
verses.transliteration:
taba āī siṃhikā bhayānī. gahi kapi chāhāṃ atula balavānī.
chaṃhā gahi tana tāsu baḍhāvā. tāhi māri kapi agama sidhāvā.
verses.translation: Then came Simhika, a demoness who caught shadows and pulled beings into the sea. She seized Hanuman's shadow.

verses.original:
मन क्रम बचन सो जतन बिचारेहु। रामचंद्र कर काजु सँवारेहु।।
भानु पीठि सेइअ उर आगी। स्वामिहि सर्ब भाव छल त्यागी।।
verses.transliteration:
hanumaṃta tāhi dekhā taba bhārī. samujhi māri tāsu tana dhārī.
laghū rūpa dhari gā mukha māhīṃ. āyau bāhara tāsu na nāhīṃ.
verses.translation: Hanuman saw her rising from the sea with a gaping mouth. He understood her trick — grow large to match her, then shrink tiny and pass through her.

verses.original:
तजि माया सेइअ परलोका। मिटहिं सकल भव संभव सोका।।
देह धरे कर यह फलु भाई। भजिअ राम सब काम बिहाई।।
verses.transliteration:
tāhi māri hanumaṃta sidhāyau. dēva sakala taba jaya jaya gāyau.
bala budhi tēja pratāpa nikāyā. rāma kāja kari sakahiṃ samāyā.
verses.translation: He killed the demoness and flew on. The sages and gods praised his cleverness: 'He who has such wit and strength will surely accomplish his mission.'

verses.original:
सोइ गुनग्य सोई बड़भागी । जो रघुबीर चरन अनुरागी।।
आयसु मागि चरन सिरु नाई। चले हरषि सुमिरत रघुराई।।
verses.transliteration:
taba dēkhī laṃkā ati sundara. trikūṭa sikhara upara mandira.
sōnē kē kōṭa aru dvārā. ratnanha jṛta ati sudara bārā.
verses.translation: At last Hanuman saw Lanka before him — a magnificent golden city atop Mount Trikuta, surrounded by the ocean. Its beauty was beyond description.

verses.original:
पाछें पवन तनय सिरु नावा। जानि काज प्रभु निकट बोलावा।।
परसा सीस सरोरुह पानी। करमुद्रिका दीन्हि जन जानी।।
verses.transliteration:
hanumaṃta taba laghū rūpa dharī. nisā samaya laṃkā pura parī.
laghū rūpa masakā kē mānā. prabhēsa kīnha atula balavānā.
verses.translation: Hanuman waited for nightfall. When darkness came, he shrank to the size of a mosquito and entered the city.

verses.original:
बहु प्रकार सीतहि समुझाएहु। कहि बल बिरह बेगि तुम्ह आएहु।।
हनुमत जन्म सुफल करि माना। चलेउ हृदयँ धरि कृपानिधाना।।
verses.transliteration:
laṃkinī milī dvāra taba āī. kauna tumha kaha kāha kahāṃ bhāī.
hanumaṃta tāhi muṣṭikā mārā. parī bhūmi mūrachā saṃbhārā.
verses.translation: At the gate he met Lankini, the guardian demoness. She challenged him. Hanuman struck her with his fist and she fell.

verses.original: जद्यपि प्रभु जानत सब बाता। राजनीति राखत सुरत्राता।।
verses.transliteration:
laṃkinī kaha sunu bānarā. jaba mohi kapi marai muṣṭi prahārā.
taba jānēhu laṃkā kā kālā. brahmā kahā mōhi yaha saba hālā.
verses.translation: Lankini recognized her fate: 'When a monkey strikes me, Lanka's end is near — so Brahma once told me. Go, mighty one. Lanka is doomed.'

verses.original:
चले सकल बन खोजत सरिता सर गिरि खोह।
राम काज लयलीन मन बिसरा तन कर छोह।।23।।
verses.transliteration:
prabhēśi nagara hanumaṃta tabahiṃ. rāvana maṃdira dēkha sabahiṃ.
sītā khōja phirata gṛha māhīṃ. dēkhī saba laṃkā tēhi nāhīṃ.
verses.translation: Thus Hanuman entered Lanka and began searching for Sita. Palace after palace he searched, but Sita was nowhere to be found.

verses.original:
कतहुँ होइ निसिचर सैं भेटा। प्रान लेहिं एक एक चपेटा।।
बहु प्रकार गिरि कानन हेरहिं। कोउ मुनि मिलत ताहि सब घेरहिं।।
verses.transliteration:
rāvana bhavana dēkhi kapi āyau. ati bicitra maṃdira mana bhāyau.
bahutā nāri tāsu gṛha sōī. sītā samāna tēhi kachu na hōī.
verses.translation: Hanuman searched every palace and mansion in Lanka. He saw Ravana's court, his queens, and his wealth — but no Sita.

verses.original:
लागि तृषा अतिसय अकुलाने। मिलइ न जल घन गहन भुलाने।।
मन हनुमान कीन्ह अनुमाना। मरन चहत सब बिनु जल पाना।।
verses.transliteration:
ēka gṛha dēkhi parama suhāvā. hari nāma taha likhā suhāvā.
tulasī taru taha dēkhā bārī. mana bichāra karata hanumaṃtā bhārī.
verses.translation: Then he saw one palace that was different — simple, with the name of Rama written everywhere. Tulasi plants grew in its garden.

verses.original:
चढ़ि गिरि सिखर चहूँ दिसि देखा। भूमि बिबिर एक कौतुक पेखा।।
चक्रबाक बक हंस उड़ाहीं। बहुतक खग प्रबिसहिं तेहि माहीं।।
verses.transliteration:
kō rāma bhagata ēhi purī. mana bichāra karā ati dūrī.
taba dēkhā taha ēka sujānā. rāma rāma japata bhagavānā.
verses.translation: Hanuman thought, 'In this city of demons, who could be a devotee of Rama?' He entered and found Vibhishana chanting the Lord's name.

verses.original:
गिरि ते उतरि पवनसुत आवा। सब कहुँ लै सोइ बिबर देखावा।।
आगें कै हनुमंतहि लीन्हा। पैठे बिबर बिलंबु न कीन्हा।।
verses.transliteration:
hanumaṃta tēhi āpuhiṃ cinā. pragaṭa bhayau taba mīta nabīnā.
bibhīṣana kaha sunu kapirāyā. sītā rākhī aśōka bana māyā.
verses.translation: Hanuman revealed himself. Vibhishana was overjoyed to meet a servant of Rama. He told Hanuman about Sita's location in the Ashoka grove.

verses.original:
दीख जाइ उपवन बर सर बिगसित बहु कंज।
मंदिर एक रुचिर तहँ बैठि नारि तप पुंज।।24।।
verses.transliteration:
sunu kapi aśōka bana sītā. rākhī taha rāvana bhaya bhītā.
jāhu turanta tāsu sudhi lāvahu. rāma carana sādara sira nāvahu.
verses.translation: Vibhishana said, 'Go to the Ashoka grove. There you will find Mother Sita, guarded by fierce demonesses. Be careful, and give her hope.'

verses.original:
दूरि ते ताहि सबन्हि सिर नावा। पूछें निज बृत्तांत सुनावा।।
तेहिं तब कहा करहु जल पाना। खाहु सुरस सुंदर फल नाना।।
verses.transliteration:
hanumaṃta kaha sunu sujānā. tuma sama rāma bhagata nahiṃ ānā.
rāma kṛpā tumha para hoī. laṃkā rāja tumhahi dēhiṃ sōī.
verses.translation: Hanuman thanked Vibhishana and said, 'You are a true devotee of Rama. When the Lord comes, He will crown you king of Lanka.'

verses.original:
मज्जनु कीन्ह मधुर फल खाए। तासु निकट पुनि सब चलि आए।।
तेहिं सब आपनि कथा सुनाई। मैं अब जाब जहाँ रघुराई।।
verses.transliteration:
bibhīṣana kaha sunu hanumaṃtā. rāma bhagati mōhi cāhia aṃtā.
laṃkā rāja nahiṃ mōhi suhāī. rāma carana sēvā mana bhāī.
verses.translation: Vibhishana was moved by these words. 'I care nothing for Lanka's throne,' he said. 'I desire only devotion to Rama's feet.'

verses.original:
मूदहु नयन बिबर तजि जाहू। पैहहु सीतहि जनि पछिताहू।।
नयन मूदि पुनि देखहिं बीरा। ठाढ़े सकल सिंधु कें तीरा।।
verses.transliteration:
calā hanumaṃta taba aśōka bārī. dēkhī sītā ati dukha bhārī.
aśōka taru tara baiṭhī sōī. kṛśa badana dukha pāvata roī.
verses.translation: Hanuman then proceeded to the Ashoka grove. Under a great Ashoka tree, he saw Sita — thin, pale, sitting on the bare ground, surrounded by fierce demonesses.

verses.original:
सो पुनि गई जहाँ रघुनाथा। जाइ कमल पद नाएसि माथा।।
नाना भाँति बिनय तेहिं कीन्ही। अनपायनी भगति प्रभु दीन्ही।।
verses.transliteration:
dēkhi janakī parama dukha pāyau. nayana nīra hanumaṃta na āyau.
taru paru chupi rahā sōcā. kaba maiṃ karauṃ mātu sana bōcā.
verses.translation: Seeing Mother Sita in such a pitiable state, Hanuman's eyes filled with tears. But he controlled himself and hid in the tree, waiting for the right moment.

verses.original:
बदरीबन कहुँ सो गई प्रभु अग्या धरि सीस ।
उर धरि राम चरन जुग जे बंदत अज ईस।।25।।
verses.transliteration:
ihāṃ samāpta kiṣkiṃdhā kāṃḍa suhāvana. āgēṃ suṃdara kāṃḍa ati pāvana.
jaya raghubīra dayāla raghunaṃdana. sītā rāma lakhaṃna baṃdana.
verses.translation: Thus ends Kishkindha Kand. Hanuman has reached Sita — and the glorious story continues in Sundar Kand, the most beautiful chapter of the Ramcharitmanas.

verses.original:
इहाँ बिचारहिं कपि मन माहीं। बीती अवधि काज कछु नाहीं।।
सब मिलि कहहिं परस्पर बाता। बिनु सुधि लएँ करब का भ्राता।।
verses.transliteration:
dakṣiṇa disi kapi saba calē. bana giri nadi bahuta mahaṃ milē.
adbhuta deśa dēkhē bahu bhāṃtī. guhā nadī taru bēli suhātī.
verses.translation: The monkeys of the southern party traveled through many lands. They saw wondrous sights — magical caves, divine rivers, and ancient forests.

verses.original:
कह अंगद लोचन भरि बारी। दुहुँ प्रकार भइ मृत्यु हमारी।।
इहाँ न सुधि सीता कै पाई। उहाँ गएँ मारिहि कपिराई।।
verses.transliteration:
khōjata phirata udāsa bahu māsā. sītā kahūṃ na milī mana trāsā.
giri guhā bana saba mahaṃ khōjā. kachu sudhi pāī nahiṃ mana sōjā.
verses.translation: They searched tirelessly through mountains and valleys. Every cave was explored, every forest scoured, but Sita remained unfound.

verses.original:
पिता बधे पर मारत मोही। राखा राम निहोर न ओही।।
पुनि पुनि अंगद कह सब पाहीं। मरन भयउ कछु संसय नाहीं।।
verses.transliteration:
avadhi nīkē bhā ati bhārī. āgē sāgara pāchē mārī.
aṃgada kaha sunu kapi samājā. phirē binu sudhi marahiṃ hama lājā.
verses.translation: The month given by Sugriva was about to expire. The monkeys grew desperate. Angad said, 'If we return without news, we shall surely be punished.'

verses.original:
अंगद बचन सुनत कपि बीरा। बोलि न सकहिं नयन बह नीरा।।
छन एक सोच मगन होइ रहे। पुनि अस वचन कहत सब भए।।
verses.transliteration:
kōū kaha prāna tajahu hama bhāī. binu sītā sudhi gṛhahi na jāī.
sabahiṃ bikala ati dukha mana māhīṃ. upāya kachu sūjhata nahiṃ tāhīṃ.
verses.translation: Some suggested they fast unto death. Others wept. It was a moment of utter despair for the entire monkey army.

verses.original:
हम सीता कै सुधि लिन्हें बिना। नहिं जैंहैं जुबराज प्रबीना।।
अस कहि लवन सिंधु तट जाई। बैठे कपि सब दर्भ डसाई।।
verses.transliteration:
tabahiṃ gṛdha ēka prabala udārā. āyau nāma saṃpāti apārā.
jaṭāyu baṃdhu suni kapi haraṣānā. tāsu kathā kahi saba pahicānā.
verses.translation: Just then the great vulture Sampati appeared. Old and wingless, he rejoiced hearing about his brother Jatayu's noble sacrifice for Rama.

verses.original:
जामवंत अंगद दुख देखी। कहिं कथा उपदेस बिसेषी।।
तात राम कहुँ नर जनि मानहु। निर्गुन ब्रम्ह अजित अज जानहु।।
verses.transliteration:
saṃpāti kaha sunu kapi sujānā. mama nayana bala atula pramānā.
sāgara pāra laṃkā mahaṃ sītā. rāvana rākhī aśōka punītā.
verses.translation: Sampati told them how he had lost his wings protecting Jatayu from the sun long ago. Though old and flightless, his divine vision could see across the ocean.

verses.original: हम सब सेवक अति बड़भागी। संतत सगुन ब्रह्म अनुरागी।।
verses.transliteration:
dēkhauṃ sītā aśōka bana māhīṃ. rāma biraha dukha sahata na jāhīṃ.
rākhī taha rākṣasinha bēṃḍāī. prabhu smaranti ati ārata bhāī.
verses.translation: 'I can see Sita in the Ashoka grove of Lanka. Ravana has imprisoned her there. She sits beneath a tree, surrounded by demonesses, yet her heart is fixed on Rama.'

verses.original:
निज इच्छा प्रभु अवतरइ सुर महि गो द्विज लागि।
सगुन उपासक संग तहँ रहहिं मोच्छ सब त्यागि।।26।।
verses.transliteration:
suni saṃpāti bacana saba haraṣē. sāgara sata jōjana ati paraṣē.
kō kapi sāgara pāra gamāvā. sabahiṃ bicāra mana mahaṃ āvā.
verses.translation: The monkeys were overjoyed. Now they knew where Sita was. But who among them could leap a hundred yojanas across the mighty ocean?

verses.original:
एहि बिधि कथा कहहि बहु भाँती गिरि कंदराँ सुनी संपाती।।
बाहेर होइ देखि बहु कीसा। मोहि अहार दीन्ह जगदीसा।।
verses.transliteration:
baiṭhē kapi saba bicāra mana kīnhā. aṃgada kaha maiṃ navati lagī nā.
jāūṃ pāra parṃ āvana nāhīṃ. yaha bidhi kāja na saranhi māhīṃ.
verses.translation: The monkeys sat pondering. One by one they stated their limits. No one could leap the full hundred yojanas. Angad could do ninety but not return.

verses.original:
आजु सबहि कहँ भच्छन करऊँ। दिन बहु चले अहार बिनु मरऊँ।।
कबहुँ न मिल भरि उदर अहारा। आजु दीन्ह बिधि एकहिं बारा।।
verses.transliteration:
jāmavaṃta taba hanumaṃta nihārā. kahā kāha baiṭhē balavānā.
tuma sama nahiṃ kōū atula balavaṃtā. uṭhahu kapi raghubaṃsa hanumaṃtā.
verses.translation: Old Jambavan smiled and looked at Hanuman. 'Why do you sit silent, O son of the Wind? You alone can accomplish this mission.'

verses.original:
डरपे गीध बचन सुनि काना। अब भा मरन सत्य हम जाना।।
कपि सब उठे गीध कहँ देखी। जामवंत मन सोच बिसेषी।।
verses.transliteration:
sunu kapi tōhi na kachu agamā. kāju na kachu tumhahi duṣkarā.
aṃjanī putra pavana suta tōhī. bala buddhi guṇa nahiṃ kachu mōhī.
verses.translation: Jambavan told Hanuman the story of his birth: son of Anjana and Vayu, blessed with divine powers. As a child he had leapt toward the sun itself.

verses.original:
कह अंगद बिचारि मन माहीं। धन्य जटायू सम कोउ नाहीं।।
राम काज कारन तनु त्यागी । हरि पुर गयउ परम बड़ भागी।।
verses.transliteration:
tumharē bala buddhi bēda na āhī. rāma kāja lagiṃ tumhahi na kāhī.
kahu kapi uṭhahu karahu aba kājā. rāma carana smarahu banarājā.
verses.translation: 'You forgot your powers due to a sage's curse. But now remember them! You can move mountains, cross oceans, and defeat any foe.'

verses.original:
सुनि खग हरष सोक जुत बानी । आवा निकट कपिन्ह भय मानी।।
तिन्हहि अभय करि पूछेसि जाई। कथा सकल तिन्ह ताहि सुनाई।।
verses.transliteration:
suni kapi mana mahaṃ bala adhikāī. tanu biṣāla pragaṭa bahu bhāī.
maṃdara samāna tēja ati bhārī. dēkhata sakala kapi ati sukhakārī.
verses.translation: As Hanuman heard these words, memories of his power flooded back. His body began to grow, glowing with golden light. The monkeys watched in amazement.

verses.original: सुनि संपाति बंधु कै करनी। रघुपति महिमा बधुबिधि बरनी।।
verses.transliteration:
hanumaṃta bōlē bala pāī. rāma kṛpā kachu durlabha nāhīṃ.
jāūṃ laṃkā sītā sudhi lāūṃ. rāma kāja kari tabahiṃ āūṃ.
verses.translation: Hanuman said, 'By the grace of Lord Rama, I shall leap across this ocean and find Mother Sita! Watch me, O monkeys — this is the power of devotion!'

verses.original:
मोहि लै जाहु सिंधुतट देउँ तिलांजलि ताहि ।
बचन सहाइ करवि मैं पैहहु खोजहु जाहि ।।27।।
verses.transliteration:
jaya jaya jaya hanumaṃta balabaṃtā. sahāya karu raghubīra kṛpā saṃtā.
kapi uṭhi dharā kanaka giri bhārī. sāgara laṃghana kīnhā apārī.
verses.translation: The monkeys cheered as Hanuman stood mighty as a golden mountain. He bowed in his heart to Rama and prepared for the greatest leap in the history of the world.

verses.original:
अनुज क्रिया करि सागर तीरा। कहि निज कथा सुनहु कपि बीरा।।
हम द्वौ बंधु प्रथम तरुनाई । गगन गए रबि निकट उडाई।।
verses.transliteration:
pada prahāri giri kūdi caḍhāyau. giri cūra bhā nabhahiṃ sidhāyau.
hanumaṃta pragaṭa prabhāva apārā. nabha mahaṃ calā ati udārā.
verses.translation: Hanuman pressed his feet against the mountain and sprang into the sky with immense force. The mountain crumbled and sank into the earth from the impact.

verses.original:
तेज न सहि सक सो फिरि आवा । मै अभिमानी रबि निअरावा ।।
जरे पंख अति तेज अपारा । परेउँ भूमि करि घोर चिकारा ।।
verses.transliteration:
bāna rāma kē dhanuṣa samānā. calā gagana mahaṃ ati balavānā.
sāgara upara calā kapirāī. dēva nabha tē puṣpa barasāī.
verses.translation: He flew through the air like an arrow released from Rama's bow. Below him stretched the vast blue ocean. Above, the gods gathered to watch.

verses.original:
मुनि एक नाम चंद्रमा ओही। लागी दया देखी करि मोही।।
बहु प्रकार तेंहि ग्यान सुनावा । देहि जनित अभिमानी छड़ावा ।।
verses.transliteration:
sāgara kaha mainaka tuma jāhū. kapi kahu bisrāma kachu dāhū.
mainaka upara kara tēhi dharā. rāma kāja binu mōhi na ṭharā.
verses.translation: The ocean god raised Mount Mainaka from beneath the waters, offering rest. Hanuman politely declined, touching the mountain and flying on.

verses.original:
त्रेताँ ब्रह्म मनुज तनु धरिही। तासु नारि निसिचर पति हरिही।।
तासु खोज पठइहि प्रभू दूता। तिन्हहि मिलें तैं होब पुनीता।।
verses.transliteration:
surasā āī paramabala dhārī. mukha pasārā jōjana bistārī.
hanumaṃta rūpa ati laghū kīnhā. mukha mahaṃ gayau āyau bāhara līnhā.
verses.translation: Next came Surasa's test. The serpent mother opened her mouth, demanding Hanuman enter. He grew enormous, she grew larger. Then he shrank tiny, entered and exited instantly.

verses.original:
जमिहहिं पंख करसि जनि चिंता । तिन्हहि देखाइ देहेसु तैं सीता।।
मुनि कइ गिरा सत्य भइ आजू । सुनि मम बचन करहु प्रभु काजू।।
verses.transliteration:
siṃhikā chāhā gahi tana tānā. kapirāja tāhi māri balavānā.
tāhi māri kapi āgēṃ gayaū. laṃkā nikaṭa samīpa taba bhayaū.
verses.translation: Then Simhika, the shadow-seizer, caught his shadow. Hanuman grew enormous, entered her mouth, and tore out her insides, slaying her.

verses.original:
गिरि त्रिकूट ऊपर बस लंका । तहँ रह रावन सहज असंका ।।
तहँ असोक उपबन जहँ रहई ।। सीता बैठि सोच रत अहई।।
verses.transliteration:
dēkhī laṃkā ati sundarāī. maṃdira kōṭa ati bicitra banāī.
nisā samaya laghū rūpa banāyā. laṃkā mahaṃ kapirāja samāyā.
verses.translation: At last, Lanka appeared before him — golden walls, jeweled towers rising above the ocean. Hanuman waited for night, then entered in tiny form.

verses.original:
मैं देखउँ तुम्ह नाहि गीघहि दष्टि अपार।।
बूढ भयउँ न त करतेउँ कछुक सहाय तुम्हार।।28।।
verses.transliteration:
laṃkā mahaṃ kapi saba gṛha dēkhā. rāma bhagata bibhīṣana lēkhā.
sītā kahāṃ tāsu sudhi pāī. aśōka bana mahaṃ rākhī jāī.
verses.translation: Hanuman entered Lanka and searched palace after palace through the night. He found Vibhishana's house, the one devotee of Rama, and learned of Sita's location.

verses.original:
जो नाघइ सत जोजन सागर । करइ सो राम काज मति आगर ।।
मोहि बिलोकि धरहु मन धीरा । राम कृपाँ कस भयउ सरीरा।।
verses.transliteration:
bibhīṣana sakala kathā sunāī. sītā dukha mahimā samujhāī.
rāvana tāhi bahuta samujhāvā. sītā mana mahaṃ rāma pada pāvā.
verses.translation: Vibhishana told Hanuman everything — how Ravana had abducted Sita, how she refused to submit, and how she sat day and night remembering Rama.

verses.original:
पापिउ जा कर नाम सुमिरहीं। अति अपार भवसागर तरहीं।।
तासु दूत तुम्ह तजि कदराई। राम हृदयँ धरि करहु उपाई।।
verses.transliteration:
aśōka bana mahaṃ prātaḥ jāhū. siṃsapā taru tara dēkhahu māhū.
rāma mudrikā dēhu taba tāhī. āśā dharahu bōlahu mana māhī.
verses.translation: 'Go to the Ashoka grove at dawn,' said Vibhishana. 'You will see her beneath the shimshapa tree. Give her Rama's ring and fill her heart with hope.'

verses.original:
अस कहि गरुड़ गीध जब गयऊ। तिन्ह कें मन अति बिसमय भयऊ।।
निज निज बल सब काहूँ भाषा। पार जाइ कर संसय राखा।।
verses.transliteration:
hanumaṃta calā aśōka banā. mana mahaṃ sītā dukha mana janā.
rāma nāma sumiri ura dharī. calā kapi ati sāvadhi karī.
verses.translation: Hanuman thanked Vibhishana and proceeded toward the Ashoka grove. His heart was heavy knowing the suffering of Mother Sita.

verses.original:
जरठ भयउँ अब कहइ रिछेसा। नहिं तन रहा प्रथम बल लेसा।।
जबहिं त्रिबिक्रम भए खरारी। तब मैं तरुन रहेउँ बल भारी।।
verses.transliteration:
dēkhī sītā taru tara baiṭhī. kṛśa tanu rāma rāma mukha ṭhaiṭhī.
rāma biraha dukha dēha sukāī. mana prabaḷa rāma pada lipṭāī.
verses.translation: In the grove, under a great tree, he saw her — frail, tearful, yet radiating an inner light. She was chanting Rama's name softly, her faith unbroken.

verses.original:
बलि बाँधत प्रभु बाढेउ सो तनु बरनि न जाई।
उभय धरी महँ दीन्ही सात प्रदच्छिन धाइ।।29।।
verses.transliteration:
hanumaṃta dēkhī sītā. kṛśa badana parama punītā.
āgēṃ suṃdara kāṃḍa rasālā. rāma kṛpā suṃdara guṇa mālā.
verses.translation: Thus ends the Kishkindha Kand, with Hanuman having found Sita in the Ashoka grove. The wondrous Sundar Kand now begins.

verses.original:
अंगद कहइ जाउँ मैं पारा। जियँ संसय कछु फिरती बारा।।
जामवंत कह तुम्ह सब लायक। पठइअ किमि सब ही कर नायक।।
verses.transliteration:
yaha kiṣkiṃdhā kāṃḍa suhāvā. rāma sugrīva mēla mana bhāvā.
bāli badha aru kapi daḷa jōrā. rāma kṛpā saba saṃśaya tōrā.
verses.translation: Blessed is this Kishkindha Kand, which tells of the divine friendship between Rama and Sugriva, the death of Vali, and the beginning of the great search.

verses.original:
कहइ रीछपति सुनु हनुमाना। का चुप साधि रहेहु बलवाना।।
पवन तनय बल पवन समाना। बुधि बिबेक बिग्यान निधाना।।
verses.transliteration:
prabhu raghubīra dīna anuragī. karata sadā āśritanha kē kājā.
bāli garaba hanumaṃta pragaṭāyau. rāma kṛpā saba jaga samujhāyau.
verses.translation: In this Kand, the Lord showed that He protects the helpless, punishes the arrogant, and rewards true devotion.

verses.original:
कवन सो काज कठिन जग माहीं। जो नहिं होइ तात तुम्ह पाहीं।।
राम काज लगि तब अवतारा। सुनतहिं भयउ पर्वताकारा।।
verses.transliteration:
jāmavaṃta budhi bala prakāśā. saṃpāti nayana dūra dēkhā āśā.
hanumaṃta bhakti jīta saba bhārī. rāma kāja kari saguna āhārī.
verses.translation: Jambavan's wisdom revealed Hanuman's forgotten strength. Sampati's vision showed them the way. And Hanuman's devotion conquered every obstacle.

verses.original:
कनक बरन तन तेज बिराजा। मानहु अपर गिरिन्ह कर राजा।।
सिंहनाद करि बारहिं बारा। लीलहीं नाषउँ जलनिधि खारा।।
verses.transliteration:
yaha pavitra kathā jō sunahiṃ sujānā. pāvahiṃ rāma kṛpā bhagavānā.
sakala maṃgala dāyaka sukhakārī. kiṣkiṃdhā kāṃḍa ati hita bhārī.
verses.translation: Those who listen to this sacred tale with devotion shall be blessed. Rama's grace removes all sorrows and fulfills all righteous desires.

verses.original:
सहित सहाय रावनहि मारी। आनउँ इहाँ त्रिकूट उपारी।।
जामवंत मैं पूँछउँ तोही। उचित सिखावनु दीजहु मोही।।
verses.transliteration:
tulasīdāsa prabhu kṛpā nihārī. gāvata rāma carita sukha bhārī.
sakala lōka maṃgala sukha dāyaka. rāma carana sēvahu sabahiṃ lāyaka.
verses.translation: Tulsidas says: by the grace of Rama, I have sung this sacred tale. May all beings find shelter at the lotus feet of Lord Rama.

verses.original:
एतना करहु तात तुम्ह जाई। सीतहि देखि कहहु सुधि आई।।
तब निज भुज बल राजिव नैना। कौतुक लागि संग कपि सेना।।
verses.transliteration:
jaya raghubīra dayāla kṛpālā. jaya sītā rāma guṇa bisālā.
lakhaṃna rāma carana rata mānā. kiṣkiṃdhā kāṃḍa karata kalyānā.
verses.translation: Glory to Lord Rama, the merciful one! Glory to Mother Sita and Lord Lakshmana! May the reading of this Kand bring peace and devotion to all.

verses.original:
कपि सेन संग सँघारि निसिचर रामु सीतहि आनिहैं।
त्रैलोक पावन सुजसु सुर मुनि नारदादि बखानिहैं।।
जो सुनत गावत कहत समुझत परम पद नर पावई।
रघुबीर पद पाथोज मधुकर दास तुलसी गावई।।
verses.transliteration:
kiṣkiṃdhā kāṃḍa samāpti ati pāvana.
sunata sakala dukha dōṣa nasāvana.
rāma kṛpā sādara jō gāvahiṃ.
tē nara bhava sāgara nahiṃ āvahiṃ.
verses.translation: The Kishkindha Kand, which destroys all sins and sorrows, is thus complete. Blessed are those who recite it with faith and devotion to Lord Rama.

verses.original:
भव भेषज रघुनाथ जसु सुनहि जे नर अरु नारि।
तिन्ह कर सकल मनोरथ सिद्ध करिहि त्रिसिरारि।।30(क)।।
नीलोत्पल तन स्याम काम कोटि सोभा अधिक।
सुनिअ तासु गुन ग्राम जासु नाम अघ खग बधिक।।30(ख)।।
verses.transliteration:
sakala sumaṃgala dāyaka raghunāyaka guṇa gāna.
sādara sunahiṃ tē tarahiṃ bhava siṃdhu binā jalajāna.
verses.translation: All auspicious blessings flow from singing the glories of Lord Raghunath. Those who listen with reverence shall cross the ocean of worldly existence without even a boat.
